question_text: Differentiate the function {tex}x^{x \cos x}+\frac{x^{2}+1}{x^{2}-1}{/tex} w.r.t. x.
question_html: Differentiate the function {tex}x^{x \cos x}+\frac{x^{2}+1}{x^{2}-1}{/tex} w.r.t. x.
solution_text: Given: {tex}\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{x} \cos \mathrm{x}}+\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}+1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}{/tex} Let {tex}y=x^{x \cos x}+\frac{x^{2}+1}{x^{2}-1}{/tex} Let y = u + v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} u = x xcos x and v = {tex}\frac{x^{2}+1}{x^{2}-1}{/tex} For, {tex}\mathrm{u}=\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{x} \cos x}{/tex} Taking log on both sides, we get {tex}\log u=\log {x} ^{x \cos x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \log u=x \cdot \cos x \cdot \log x{/tex} Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{d}{d x}(\log u)=\frac{d}{d x}[x \cdot \cos x \cdot \log x]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{u}} \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\cos \mathrm{x} \cdot \log \mathrm{x} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{x} \cdot \log \mathrm{x} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\cos \mathrm{x})+\mathrm{x} \cdot \cos \mathrm{x} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{u}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{u}\left[\cos \mathrm{x} . \log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{x} \cdot \log \mathrm{x}(-\sin \mathrm{x})+\mathrm{x} \cdot \cos \mathrm{x} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=x^{x \cos x}[\cos x \cdot \log x-x \cdot \log x \cdot \sin x+\cos x]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=x^{x \cos x}[\cos x(1+\log x)-x \cdot \log x \cdot \sin x]{/tex} For, v = {tex}\frac{x^{2}+1}{x^{2}-1}{/tex} Takinglog on both sides, we get {tex}\log \mathrm{v}=\log \left(\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}+1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \log v=\log \left(x^{2}+1\right)-\log \left(x^{2}-1\right){/tex} Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{d}{d x}(\log v)=\frac{d}{d x}\left[\log \left(x^{2}+1\right)-\log \left(x^{2}-1\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}} \frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}+1} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}\right)-\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=v \cdot\left[\frac{1}{x^{2}+1} \cdot(2 x)-\frac{1}{x^{2}-1} \cdot(2 x)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=\left(\frac{x^{2}+1}{x^{2}-1}\right) \cdot\left[\frac{2 x\left(x^{2}-1\right)-2 x\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}-1\right)}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=\left(\frac{x^{2}+1}{x^{2}-1}\right) \cdot\left[\frac{-4 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}-1\right)}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=\left[\frac{-4 x}{\left(x^{2}-1\right)^{2}}\right]{/tex} Because, y = u + v {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=x^{x \cos x}[\cos x(1+\log x)-x \cdot \log x \cdot \sin x]-\left[\frac{4 x}{\left(x^{2}-1\right)^{2}}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{x} \cos \mathrm{x}}+\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}+1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}{/tex} 
Let {tex}y=x^{x \cos x}+\frac{x^{2}+1}{x^{2}-1}{/tex} 
Let y = u + v
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} u = xxcos x and v = {tex}\frac{x^{2}+1}{x^{2}-1}{/tex}
For, {tex}\mathrm{u}=\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{x} \cos x}{/tex} 
Taking log on both sides, we get
{tex}\log u=\log {x} ^{x \cos x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \log u=x \cdot \cos x \cdot \log x{/tex} 
Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x
{tex}\frac{d}{d x}(\log u)=\frac{d}{d x}[x \cdot \cos x \cdot \log x]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{u}} \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\cos \mathrm{x} \cdot \log \mathrm{x} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{x} \cdot \log \mathrm{x} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\cos \mathrm{x})+\mathrm{x} \cdot \cos \mathrm{x} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{u}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{u}\left[\cos \mathrm{x} . \log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{x} \cdot \log \mathrm{x}(-\sin \mathrm{x})+\mathrm{x} \cdot \cos \mathrm{x} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=x^{x \cos x}[\cos x \cdot \log x-x \cdot \log x \cdot \sin x+\cos x]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=x^{x \cos x}[\cos x(1+\log x)-x \cdot \log x \cdot \sin x]{/tex} 
For, v = {tex}\frac{x^{2}+1}{x^{2}-1}{/tex} 
Takinglog on both sides, we get
{tex}\log \mathrm{v}=\log \left(\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}+1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \log v=\log \left(x^{2}+1\right)-\log \left(x^{2}-1\right){/tex} 
Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x
{tex}\frac{d}{d x}(\log v)=\frac{d}{d x}\left[\log \left(x^{2}+1\right)-\log \left(x^{2}-1\right)\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}} \frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}+1} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}\right)-\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=v \cdot\left[\frac{1}{x^{2}+1} \cdot(2 x)-\frac{1}{x^{2}-1} \cdot(2 x)\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=\left(\frac{x^{2}+1}{x^{2}-1}\right) \cdot\left[\frac{2 x\left(x^{2}-1\right)-2 x\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}-1\right)}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=\left(\frac{x^{2}+1}{x^{2}-1}\right) \cdot\left[\frac{-4 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}-1\right)}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=\left[\frac{-4 x}{\left(x^{2}-1\right)^{2}}\right]{/tex} 
Because, y = u + v
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=x^{x \cos x}[\cos x(1+\log x)-x \cdot \log x \cdot \sin x]-\left[\frac{4 x}{\left(x^{2}-1\right)^{2}}\right]{/tex}

question_html: Differentiate the function {tex}{\left( {x\cos x} \right)^x} + {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}}{/tex} w.r.t. x.
solution_text: Let {tex}y = {\left( {x\cos x} \right)^x} + {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}}{/tex} Putting u = (x cos x) x and {tex}v = {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}}{/tex} we have y = u + v {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{du}}{{dx}} + \frac{{dv}}{{dx}}{/tex} ....(i) Now u = (x cos x) x {tex}\Rightarrow \log u = \log {\left( {x\cos x} \right)^x} = x\log \left( {x\cos x} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \log u = x\left( {\log x + \log \cos x} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\log u = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left\{ {x\left( {\log x + \log \cos x} \right)} \right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{u}\frac{{du}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex} = x\left[ {\frac{1}{x} + \frac{1}{{\cos x}}.\left( { - \sin x} \right)} \right] + \left( {\log x + \log \cos x} \right).1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{u}\frac{{du}}{{dx}} {/tex} {tex} = \left[ {1 - x\tan x + \log \left( {x\cos x} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{du}}{{dx}} = u\left[ {1 - x\tan x + \log \left( {x\cos x} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{du}}{{dx}} = {\left( {x\cos x} \right)^x}\left[ {1 - x\tan x + \log \left( {x\cos x} \right)} \right]{/tex} .....(ii) Again {tex}v = {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \log v = \log {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}} = \frac{1}{x}\log \left( {x\sin x} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \log v = \frac{1}{x}\left( {\log x + \log \sin x} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\log v = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left\{ {\frac{1}{x}\left( {\log x + \log \sin x} \right)} \right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{v}\frac{dv}{{dx}} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{x}\left[ {\frac{1}{x} + \frac{1}{{\ sinx }}.\cos x} \right] + \left( {\log x + \log \sin x} \right)\left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{{{x^2}}}} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{v}\frac{dv}{{dx}} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}} + \frac{{\cot x}}{x} - \frac{{\log \left( {x\sin x} \right)}}{{{x^2}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{dv}{{dx}} = v\left[ {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}} + \frac{{\cot x}}{x} - \frac{{\log \left( {x\sin x} \right)}}{{{x^2}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{dv}{{dx}} = {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}}\left[ {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}} + \frac{{\cot x}}{x} - \frac{{\log \left( {x\sin x} \right)}}{{{x^2}}}} \right]{/tex} ...(iii) Putting the values from eq. (ii) and (iii) in eq. (i) {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}} = {\left( {x\cos x} \right)^{^x}}{/tex} {tex}\left[ {1 - x\tan x + \log \left( {x\cos x} \right)} \right] + {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}}{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}} + \frac{{\cot x}}{x} - \frac{{\log \left( {x\sin x} \right)}}{{{x^2}}}} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}y = {\left( {x\cos x} \right)^x} + {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}}{/tex}

Putting u = (x cos x)x and {tex}v = {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}}{/tex}

we have y = u + v

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{du}}{{dx}} + \frac{{dv}}{{dx}}{/tex} ....(i)

Now u = (x cos x)x

{tex}\Rightarrow \log u = \log {\left( {x\cos x} \right)^x} = x\log \left( {x\cos x} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \log u = x\left( {\log x + \log \cos x} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\log u = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left\{ {x\left( {\log x + \log \cos x} \right)} \right\}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{u}\frac{{du}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex} = x\left[ {\frac{1}{x} + \frac{1}{{\cos x}}.\left( { - \sin x} \right)} \right] + \left( {\log x + \log \cos x} \right).1{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{u}\frac{{du}}{{dx}} {/tex}{tex} = \left[ {1 - x\tan x + \log \left( {x\cos x} \right)} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{du}}{{dx}} = u\left[ {1 - x\tan x + \log \left( {x\cos x} \right)} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{du}}{{dx}} = {\left( {x\cos x} \right)^x}\left[ {1 - x\tan x + \log \left( {x\cos x} \right)} \right]{/tex} .....(ii)

Again {tex}v = {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \log v = \log {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}} = \frac{1}{x}\log \left( {x\sin x} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \log v = \frac{1}{x}\left( {\log x + \log \sin x} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\log v = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left\{ {\frac{1}{x}\left( {\log x + \log \sin x} \right)} \right\}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{v}\frac{dv}{{dx}} {/tex}{tex}= \frac{1}{x}\left[ {\frac{1}{x} + \frac{1}{{\ sinx }}.\cos x} \right] + \left( {\log x + \log \sin x} \right)\left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{{{x^2}}}} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{v}\frac{dv}{{dx}} {/tex}{tex}= \left[ {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}} + \frac{{\cot x}}{x} - \frac{{\log \left( {x\sin x} \right)}}{{{x^2}}}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{dv}{{dx}} = v\left[ {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}} + \frac{{\cot x}}{x} - \frac{{\log \left( {x\sin x} \right)}}{{{x^2}}}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{dv}{{dx}} = {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}}\left[ {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}} + \frac{{\cot x}}{x} - \frac{{\log \left( {x\sin x} \right)}}{{{x^2}}}} \right]{/tex} ...(iii)

Putting the values from eq. (ii) and (iii) in eq. (i)

{tex}\frac{d}{{dx}} = {\left( {x\cos x} \right)^{^x}}{/tex}{tex}\left[ {1 - x\tan x + \log \left( {x\cos x} \right)} \right] + {\left( {x\sin x} \right)^{\frac{1}{x}}}{/tex}{tex}\left[ {\frac{1}{{{x^2}}} + \frac{{\cot x}}{x} - \frac{{\log \left( {x\sin x} \right)}}{{{x^2}}}} \right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} of the function x y + y x = 1
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} of the function xy + yx = 1
solution_text: Given: x y + y x = 1 Let y = x y + y x = 1 Let u = x y and v = y x Then, u + v = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}=0{/tex} For, u = x y Taking log on both sides, we get log u = log x y {tex}\Rightarrow \log u=y \cdot \log (x){/tex} Now, differentiating both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{u})=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}[\mathrm{y} \cdot \log (\mathrm{x})]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{u}} \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\left\{\mathrm{y} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{x})+\log \mathrm{x} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{y})\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=u\left[y \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\log x \cdot\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{y}}\left[\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}+\log \mathrm{x} \cdot\left(\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}\right)\right]{/tex} For v = y x Taking log on both sides, we get log v = log y x {tex}\Rightarrow \log v=x \cdot \log (y){/tex} Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{v})=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}[\mathrm{x} \cdot \log (\mathrm{y})]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\left\{\mathrm{x} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{y})+\log \mathrm{y} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \mathrm{x}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=v\left[x \cdot \frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+\log y \cdot\left(\frac{d x}{d x}\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=y^{x}\left[\frac{x}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+\log y\right]{/tex} because, {tex}\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}=0{/tex} So, {tex}\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{y}}\left[\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}+\log \mathrm{x} \cdot\left(\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}\right)\right]+\mathrm{y}^{\mathrm{x}}\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\log \mathrm{y}\right]=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left(\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{y}} \log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{xy}^{\mathrm{x}-1}\right) \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\left(\mathrm{yx}^{\mathrm{y}-1}+\mathrm{y}^{\mathrm{x}} \log \mathrm{y}\right)=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left(\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{y}} \log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{xy}^{\mathrm{x}-1}\right) \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\left(\mathrm{yx}^{\mathrm{y}-1}+\mathrm{y}^{\mathrm{x}} \log \mathrm{y}\right){/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{\left(y x^{y-1}+y^{x} \log y\right)}{\left(x^{y} \log x+x y^{x-1}\right)}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: xy + yx = 1
Let y = xy + yx = 1
Let u = xy and v = yx
Then, u + v = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}=0{/tex} 
For, u = xy 
Taking log on both sides, we get
log u = log xy 
{tex}\Rightarrow \log u=y \cdot \log (x){/tex} 
Now, differentiating both sides with respect to x
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{u})=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}[\mathrm{y} \cdot \log (\mathrm{x})]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{u}} \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\left\{\mathrm{y} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{x})+\log \mathrm{x} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{y})\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=u\left[y \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\log x \cdot\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{y}}\left[\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}+\log \mathrm{x} \cdot\left(\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}\right)\right]{/tex} 
For v = yx 
Taking log on both sides, we get
log v = log yx  
{tex}\Rightarrow \log v=x \cdot \log (y){/tex} 
Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{v})=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}[\mathrm{x} \cdot \log (\mathrm{y})]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\left\{\mathrm{x} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{y})+\log \mathrm{y} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \mathrm{x}\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=v\left[x \cdot \frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+\log y \cdot\left(\frac{d x}{d x}\right)\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=y^{x}\left[\frac{x}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+\log y\right]{/tex} 
because, {tex}\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}=0{/tex} 
So, {tex}\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{y}}\left[\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}+\log \mathrm{x} \cdot\left(\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}\right)\right]+\mathrm{y}^{\mathrm{x}}\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\log \mathrm{y}\right]=0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{y}} \log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{xy}^{\mathrm{x}-1}\right) \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\left(\mathrm{yx}^{\mathrm{y}-1}+\mathrm{y}^{\mathrm{x}} \log \mathrm{y}\right)=0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{y}} \log \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{xy}^{\mathrm{x}-1}\right) \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-\left(\mathrm{yx}^{\mathrm{y}-1}+\mathrm{y}^{\mathrm{x}} \log \mathrm{y}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{\left(y x^{y-1}+y^{x} \log y\right)}{\left(x^{y} \log x+x y^{x-1}\right)}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} , of the function y x = x y
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}, of the function yx = xy
solution_text: Given: y x = x y {tex} \Rightarrow {x^y} = {y^x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \log {x^y} = \log {y^x}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow y\log x = x\log y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {y\log x} \right) = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {x\log y} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y.\frac{1}{x} + \log x.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = x.\frac{1}{y}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + \log y.1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( {\log x - \frac{x}{y}} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \log y - \frac{y}{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( {\frac{{y\log x - x}}{y}} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{x\log y - y}}{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{y\left( {x\log y - y} \right)}}{{x\left( {y\log x - x} \right)}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: yx = xy

{tex} \Rightarrow {x^y} = {y^x}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \log {x^y} = \log {y^x}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow y\log x = x\log y{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {y\log x} \right) = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {x\log y} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow y.\frac{1}{x} + \log x.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = x.\frac{1}{y}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + \log y.1{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left( {\log x - \frac{x}{y}} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \log y - \frac{y}{x}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left( {\frac{{y\log x - x}}{y}} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{x\log y - y}}{x}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{y\left( {x\log y - y} \right)}}{{x\left( {y\log x - x} \right)}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find {tex}\frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} of the function (cos x) y = (cos y) x .
question_html: Find {tex}\frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} of the function (cos x)y = (cos y)x.
solution_text: We have, (cos x) y = (cos y) x On taking log both sides, we get log(cos x) y = log(cos y) x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log(cos x) = x log(cos y) On differentiating both sides w.r.t x, we get {tex}y \cdot \frac { d } { d x } \log ( \cos x ) + \log \cos x \cdot \frac { d } { d x } ( y ){/tex} {tex}= x \frac { d } { d x } \log \left( \cos y) + \log ( \cos y ) \frac { d } { d x } ( x )\right.{/tex} [by using product rule of derivative] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad y \cdot \frac { 1 } { \cos x } \frac { d } { d x } ( \cos x ) + \log ( \cos x ) \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} {tex}= x \cdot \frac { 1 } { \cos y } \frac { d } { d x }{/tex} (cos y) + log cos y.1 {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot \frac { 1 } { \cos x } ( - \sin x ) + \log ( \cos x ) \cdot \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} {tex}= x \cdot \frac { 1 } { \cos y } {/tex} (-sin y) {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} + log cos y.1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - y tanx + log(cos x) {tex}\frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} =-x tan y {tex}\frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} + log(cos y) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} [ x tan y + log (cos x)] {tex}​​\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = log(cos y) + y tan x {tex}\therefore \quad \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { \log ( \cos y ) + y \tan x } { x \tan y + \log ( \cos x ) }{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, (cos x)y = (cos y)x

On taking log both sides, we get
log(cos x)y = log(cos y)x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log(cos x) = x log(cos y)
On differentiating both sides w.r.t x, we get
{tex}y \cdot \frac { d } { d x } \log ( \cos x ) + \log \cos x \cdot \frac { d } { d x } ( y ){/tex}
{tex}= x \frac { d } { d x } \log \left( \cos y) + \log ( \cos y ) \frac { d } { d x } ( x )\right.{/tex} [by using product rule of derivative]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad y \cdot \frac { 1 } { \cos x } \frac { d } { d x } ( \cos x ) + \log ( \cos x ) \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex}{tex}= x \cdot \frac { 1 } { \cos y } \frac { d } { d x }{/tex}(cos y) + log cos y.1
{tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot \frac { 1 } { \cos x } ( - \sin x ) + \log ( \cos x ) \cdot \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex}{tex}= x \cdot \frac { 1 } { \cos y } {/tex}(-sin y){tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} + log cos y.1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - y tanx + log(cos x){tex}\frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} =-x tan y{tex}\frac { d y } { d x }{/tex}+ log(cos y)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}[ x tan y + log (cos x)]{tex}​​\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}= log(cos y) + y tan x
{tex}\therefore \quad \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { \log ( \cos y ) + y \tan x } { x \tan y + \log ( \cos x ) }{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} of the function xy = e (x – y)
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} of the function xy = e(x – y)
solution_text: Given: xy = e (x – y) Taking log on both sides, we get log (x y) = log (e (x – y) ) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log x + log y = (x - y) log e {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log x + log y = (x - y) .1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log x + log y = (x - y) Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \mathrm{x}+\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \mathrm{y}=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \mathrm{x}-\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \mathrm{y}{/tex} {tex}\implies{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=1-\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\implies{/tex} {tex}\left(1+\frac{1}{y}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}=1-\frac{1}{x}{/tex} {tex}\implies{/tex} {tex}\frac{1+y}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x-1}{x}{/tex} {tex}\implies{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y(x-1)}{x(1+y)}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: xy = e(x – y)
Taking log on both sides, we get
log (x y) = log (e(x – y))
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log x + log y = (x - y) log e
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log x + log y = (x - y) .1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log x + log y = (x - y)
Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \mathrm{x}+\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \mathrm{y}=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \mathrm{x}-\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \mathrm{y}{/tex}
{tex}\implies{/tex}  {tex}\frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=1-\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
{tex}\implies{/tex}{tex}\left(1+\frac{1}{y}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}=1-\frac{1}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\implies{/tex}{tex}\frac{1+y}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x-1}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\implies{/tex}{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y(x-1)}{x(1+y)}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the derivative of the function given by f(x) = (1 + x) (1 + x 2 ) (1 + x 4 ) (1 + x 8 ) and hence find f ′(1).
question_html: Find the derivative of the function given by f(x) = (1 + x) (1 + x2) (1 + x4) (1 + x8) and hence find f ′(1).
solution_text: Given: f(x) = (1 + x)(1 + x 2 )(1 + x 4 )(1 + x 8 ) Taking log on both sides, we get log f(x) = log (1 + x) + log(1 + x 2 ) + log (1 + x 4 ) + log (1 + x 8 ) Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log (1+\mathrm{x})+\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \left(1+\mathrm{x}^{2}\right)+\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \left(1+\mathrm{x}^{4}\right)+\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \left(1+\mathrm{x}^{8}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{f(x)} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}[f(x)]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(1+\mathrm{x})+\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}^{2}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(1+\mathrm{x}^{2}\right)+\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}^{4}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(1+\mathrm{x}^{4}\right){/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{1+x^{8}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(1+x^{8}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})\left[\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}}+\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}^{2}} \cdot(2 \mathrm{x})+\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}^{4}} \cdot\left(4 \mathrm{x}^{3}\right)+\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}^{8}} \cdot\left(8 \mathrm{x}^{7}\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=(1+\mathrm{x})\left(1+\mathrm{x}^{2}\right)\left(1+\mathrm{x}^{4}\right)\left(1+\mathrm{x}^{8}\right)\left[\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}}+\frac{2 \mathrm{x}}{1+\mathrm{x}^{2}}+\frac{4 \mathrm{x}^{3}}{1+\mathrm{x}^{4}}+\frac{8 \mathrm{x}^{7}}{1+\mathrm{x}^{8}}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(1)=(1+1)\left(1+1^{2}\right)\left(1+1^{4}\right)(1+1^8){/tex} {tex}\left[\frac{1}{1+1}+\frac{2(1)}{1+1}+\frac{4(1)^{3}}{1+(1)^{4}}+\frac{8(1)^{7}}{1+(1)^{8}}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(1)=(2)(2)(2)(2)\left[\frac{1}{2}+\frac{2}{2}+\frac{4}{2}+\frac{8}{2}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(1)=16\left(\frac{1+2+4+8}{2}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(1)=16\left(\frac{15}{2}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(1)=120{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: f(x) = (1 + x)(1 + x2)(1 + x4)(1 + x8)
Taking log on both sides, we get
log f(x) = log (1 + x) + log(1 + x2) + log (1 + x4) + log (1 + x8)
Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log (1+\mathrm{x})+\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \left(1+\mathrm{x}^{2}\right)+\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \left(1+\mathrm{x}^{4}\right)+\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \left(1+\mathrm{x}^{8}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{f(x)} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}[f(x)]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(1+\mathrm{x})+\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}^{2}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(1+\mathrm{x}^{2}\right)+\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}^{4}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(1+\mathrm{x}^{4}\right){/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{1+x^{8}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(1+x^{8}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})\left[\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}}+\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}^{2}} \cdot(2 \mathrm{x})+\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}^{4}} \cdot\left(4 \mathrm{x}^{3}\right)+\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}^{8}} \cdot\left(8 \mathrm{x}^{7}\right)\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=(1+\mathrm{x})\left(1+\mathrm{x}^{2}\right)\left(1+\mathrm{x}^{4}\right)\left(1+\mathrm{x}^{8}\right)\left[\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}}+\frac{2 \mathrm{x}}{1+\mathrm{x}^{2}}+\frac{4 \mathrm{x}^{3}}{1+\mathrm{x}^{4}}+\frac{8 \mathrm{x}^{7}}{1+\mathrm{x}^{8}}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(1)=(1+1)\left(1+1^{2}\right)\left(1+1^{4}\right)(1+1^8){/tex} {tex}\left[\frac{1}{1+1}+\frac{2(1)}{1+1}+\frac{4(1)^{3}}{1+(1)^{4}}+\frac{8(1)^{7}}{1+(1)^{8}}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(1)=(2)(2)(2)(2)\left[\frac{1}{2}+\frac{2}{2}+\frac{4}{2}+\frac{8}{2}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(1)=16\left(\frac{1+2+4+8}{2}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(1)=16\left(\frac{15}{2}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(1)=120{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Differentiate (x 2 - 5x + 8) (x 3 + 7x + 9) in three ways mentioned below: by using product rule by expanding the product to obtain a single polynomial by logarithmic differentiation. Do they all give the same answer?
question_html:
Differentiate (x2 - 5x + 8) (x3 + 7x + 9) in three ways mentioned below:

  1. by using product rule
  2. by expanding the product to obtain a single polynomial
  3. by logarithmic differentiation.
    Do they all give the same answer?
solution_text: Given: (x 2 – 5x + 8) (x 3 + 7x + 9) Let y = (x 2 – 5x + 8) (x 3 + 7x + 9) By applying product rule differentiate both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (x 2 − 5x + 8)(x 3 + 7x + 9) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right){/tex} + {tex}\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right) \cdot(2 x-5)+\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right) \cdot\left(3 x^{2}+7\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}=2{x}^{4} +14 {x}^{2}+18 \mathrm{x}-5{x}^{3} {/tex} - 35 {tex}x{/tex} − 45 + 3 {tex}x{/tex} 4 + 7 {tex}x{/tex} 2 − 15 {tex}x{/tex} 3 − 35 {tex}x{/tex} + 24 {tex}x{/tex} 2 + 56 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = 5x 4 − 20x 3 + 45x 2 − 52x + 11 Given: (x 2 – 5x + 8) (x 3 + 7x + 9) Let y = (x 2 – 5x + 8) (x 3 + 7x + 9) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = (x 2 – 5x + 8) (x 3 + 7x + 9) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = x 5 + 7x 3 + 9x 2 - 5x 4 – 35x 2 - 45x + 8x 3 + 56x + 72 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = x 5 - 5x 4 + 15x 3 - 26x 2 + 11x + 72 Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{5}\right)-\frac{d}{d x}\left(5 x^{4}\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(15 x^{3}\right)-\frac{d}{d x}\left(26 x^{2}\right)+\frac{d}{d x}(11 x)+\frac{d}{d x}(72){/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = 5x 4 - 20x 3 + 45x 2 - 52x + 11 Given: (x 2 – 5x + 8) (x 3 + 7x + 9) Let y = (x 2 – 5x + 8) (x 3 + 7x + 9) Taking log on both sides, we get log y = log ((x 2 – 5x + 8) (x 3 + 7x + 9)) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log y = log (x 2 – 5x + 8) + log (x 3 + 7x + 9) Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{y})=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \left(\mathrm{x}^{2}-5 \mathrm{x}+8\right)+\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \left(\mathrm{x}^{3}+7 \mathrm{x}+9\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{y}} \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{y})=\left[\frac{1}{\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}-5 \mathrm{x}+8\right)} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}-5 \mathrm{x}+8\right)+\frac{1}{\left(\mathrm{x}^{3}+7 \mathrm{x}+9\right)} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{3}+7 \mathrm{x}+9\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{y} \frac{d}{d x}(y)=\left[\frac{1}{\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)} \cdot(2 x-5)+\frac{1}{\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right)} \cdot\left(3 x^{2}+7\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(y)=y \cdot\left[\frac{(2 x-5)}{\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)}+\frac{\left(3 x^{2}+7\right)}{\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right)}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(y)=y \cdot\left[\frac{(2 x-5)\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right)+\left(3 x^{2}+7\right)\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)}{\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right)}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(y){/tex} {tex}=y \cdot\left[\frac{2 x^{4}+14 x^{2}+18 x-5 x^{3}-35 x-45+3 x^{4}-15 x^{3}+24 x^{2}+7 x^{2}-35 x+56}{\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right)}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(y)=\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right){/tex} . {tex}\left[\frac{5 x^{4}-20 x^{3}-45 x^{2}-52 x+11}{\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right)}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = 5x 4 - 20x 3 + 45x 2 - 52x + 11
solution_html:
1. Given: (x2 – 5x + 8) (x3 + 7x + 9)
    Let y = (x2 – 5x + 8) (x3 + 7x + 9)
    By applying product rule differentiate both sides with respect to x
    {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}{/tex}(x2 − 5x + 8)(x3 + 7x + 9)
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right){/tex} + {tex}\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right){/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right) \cdot(2 x-5)+\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right) \cdot\left(3 x^{2}+7\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}=2{x}^{4} +14 {x}^{2}+18 \mathrm{x}-5{x}^{3} {/tex}- 35{tex}x{/tex} − 45 + 3{tex}x{/tex}4 + 7{tex}x{/tex}2 − 15{tex}x{/tex}3 − 35{tex}x{/tex}+ 24{tex}x{/tex}2 + 56
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}= 5x4 − 20x3 + 45x2 − 52x + 11
  2. Given: (x2 – 5x + 8) (x3 + 7x + 9)
    Let y = (x2 – 5x + 8) (x3 + 7x + 9)
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}y = (x2 – 5x + 8) (x3 + 7x + 9)
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}y = x5 + 7x3 + 9x2 - 5x4 – 35x2 - 45x + 8x3 + 56x + 72
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}y = x5 - 5x4 + 15x3 - 26x2 + 11x + 72
    Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x, we get
    {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{5}\right)-\frac{d}{d x}\left(5 x^{4}\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(15 x^{3}\right)-\frac{d}{d x}\left(26 x^{2}\right)+\frac{d}{d x}(11 x)+\frac{d}{d x}(72){/tex}
    {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = 5x4 - 20x3 + 45x2 - 52x + 11  
  3. Given: (x2 – 5x + 8) (x3 + 7x + 9)
    Let y = (x2 – 5x + 8) (x3 + 7x + 9)
    Taking log on both sides, we get
    log y = log ((x2 – 5x + 8) (x3 + 7x + 9))
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log y = log (x2 – 5x + 8) + log (x3 + 7x + 9)
    Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x
    {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{y})=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \left(\mathrm{x}^{2}-5 \mathrm{x}+8\right)+\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \log \left(\mathrm{x}^{3}+7 \mathrm{x}+9\right){/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{y}} \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{y})=\left[\frac{1}{\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}-5 \mathrm{x}+8\right)} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}-5 \mathrm{x}+8\right)+\frac{1}{\left(\mathrm{x}^{3}+7 \mathrm{x}+9\right)} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{3}+7 \mathrm{x}+9\right)\right]{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{y} \frac{d}{d x}(y)=\left[\frac{1}{\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)} \cdot(2 x-5)+\frac{1}{\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right)} \cdot\left(3 x^{2}+7\right)\right]{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(y)=y \cdot\left[\frac{(2 x-5)}{\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)}+\frac{\left(3 x^{2}+7\right)}{\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right)}\right]{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(y)=y \cdot\left[\frac{(2 x-5)\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right)+\left(3 x^{2}+7\right)\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)}{\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right)}\right]{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(y){/tex}{tex}=y \cdot\left[\frac{2 x^{4}+14 x^{2}+18 x-5 x^{3}-35 x-45+3 x^{4}-15 x^{3}+24 x^{2}+7 x^{2}-35 x+56}{\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right)}\right]{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(y)=\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right){/tex}.{tex}\left[\frac{5 x^{4}-20 x^{3}-45 x^{2}-52 x+11}{\left(x^{2}-5 x+8\right)\left(x^{3}+7 x+9\right)}\right]{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = 5x4 - 20x3 + 45x2 - 52x + 11

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If u, v and w are functions of x, then show that {tex}\frac{d}{d x}(u . v . w)=\frac{d u}{d x} v . w+u . \frac{d v}{d x} \cdot w+u \cdot v \frac{d w}{d x}{/tex} in two ways - first by repeated application of product rule, second by logarithmic differentiation.
question_html:
If u, v and w are functions of x, then show that 
{tex}\frac{d}{d x}(u . v . w)=\frac{d u}{d x} v . w+u . \frac{d v}{d x} \cdot w+u \cdot v \frac{d w}{d x}{/tex} 
in two ways - first by repeated application of product rule, second by logarithmic differentiation.
solution_text: To prove: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{u} . \mathrm{v} . \mathrm{w})=\frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}} \mathrm{v} \cdot \mathrm{w}+\mathrm{u} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}} \cdot \mathrm{w}+\mathrm{u.v.} \frac{\mathrm{dw}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} Let y = u.v.w = u.(v.w) By applying product rule differentiate both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=(v \cdot w) \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}+u \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(v \cdot w){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=(v \cdot w) \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}+u \cdot\left[v \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(w)+w \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(v)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=(v \cdot w) \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}+(u \cdot v) \cdot \frac{d w}{d x}+(u . w) \cdot \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} Taking log on both sides, we get as, y = u.v.w log y = log (u.v.w) log y = log u + log v + log w Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(\log y)=\frac{d}{d x} \log u+\frac{d}{d x} \log v+\frac{d}{d x} \log w{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{y}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{y})=\frac{1}{\mathrm{u}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{u})+\frac{1}{\mathrm{v}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{v})+\frac{1}{\mathrm{w}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{w}){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(y)=y\left[\frac{1}{u} \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{1}{v} \cdot \frac{d v}{d x}+\frac{1}{w} \cdot \frac{d w}{d x}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=u . v . w\left[\frac{1}{u} \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{1}{v} \cdot \frac{d v}{d x}+\frac{1}{w} \cdot \frac{d w}{d x}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{v} \cdot \mathrm{w} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\mathrm{u.w} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\mathrm{u.v.} \frac{\mathrm{dw}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}
solution_html:
To prove: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{u} . \mathrm{v} . \mathrm{w})=\frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}} \mathrm{v} \cdot \mathrm{w}+\mathrm{u} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}} \cdot \mathrm{w}+\mathrm{u.v.} \frac{\mathrm{dw}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} 
Let y = u.v.w = u.(v.w)

  1. By applying product rule differentiate both sides with respect to x
    {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=(v \cdot w) \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}+u \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(v \cdot w){/tex} 
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=(v \cdot w) \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}+u \cdot\left[v \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(w)+w \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(v)\right]{/tex} 
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=(v \cdot w) \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}+(u \cdot v) \cdot \frac{d w}{d x}+(u . w) \cdot \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}
  2. Taking log on both sides, we get
    as, y = u.v.w
    log y = log (u.v.w)
    log y = log u + log v + log w
    Now, differentiate both sides with respect to x
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(\log y)=\frac{d}{d x} \log u+\frac{d}{d x} \log v+\frac{d}{d x} \log w{/tex} 
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{y}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{y})=\frac{1}{\mathrm{u}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{u})+\frac{1}{\mathrm{v}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{v})+\frac{1}{\mathrm{w}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{w}){/tex} 
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(y)=y\left[\frac{1}{u} \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{1}{v} \cdot \frac{d v}{d x}+\frac{1}{w} \cdot \frac{d w}{d x}\right]{/tex} 
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=u . v . w\left[\frac{1}{u} \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{1}{v} \cdot \frac{d v}{d x}+\frac{1}{w} \cdot \frac{d w}{d x}\right]{/tex} 
    {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{v} \cdot \mathrm{w} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\mathrm{u.w} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\mathrm{u.v.} \frac{\mathrm{dw}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation x = 2at 2 , y = at 4 , without eliminating the parameter, Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{/tex}
question_html: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation x = 2at2, y = at4, without eliminating the parameter, Find{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{/tex}
solution_text: Given: x = 2at 2 and y = at 4 {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {2a{t^2}} \right){/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {a{t^4}} \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = 2a\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {{t^2}} \right) = 2a.2t = 4at{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = a\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {{t^4}} \right) = a.4{t^3} = 4a{t^3}{/tex} Now {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{4a{t^3}}}{{4at}} = {t^2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: x = 2at2 and y = at4

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {2a{t^2}} \right){/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {a{t^4}} \right){/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = 2a\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {{t^2}} \right) = 2a.2t = 4at{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = a\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {{t^4}} \right) = a.4{t^3} = 4a{t^3}{/tex}

Now {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{4a{t^3}}}{{4at}} = {t^2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation {tex}x = a\cos \theta ,y = b\cos \theta{/tex} , without eliminating the parameter, Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{/tex}
question_html: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation {tex}x = a\cos \theta ,y = b\cos \theta{/tex}, without eliminating the parameter, Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{/tex}
solution_text: Given: {tex}x = a\cos \theta{/tex} and {tex}y = b\cos \theta{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {a\cos \theta } \right){/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {b\cos \theta } \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = a\frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {\cos \theta } \right){/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = b\frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {\cos \theta } \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = - a\sin \theta {/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = - b\sin \theta{/tex} Now {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/d\theta }}{{dx/d\theta }} = - \frac{{ - b\sin \theta }}{{ - a\sin \theta }} = \frac{b}{a}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}x = a\cos \theta{/tex} and {tex}y = b\cos \theta{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {a\cos \theta } \right){/tex}and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {b\cos \theta } \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = a\frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {\cos \theta } \right){/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = b\frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {\cos \theta } \right){/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = - a\sin \theta {/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = - b\sin \theta{/tex}

Now {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/d\theta }}{{dx/d\theta }} = - \frac{{ - b\sin \theta }}{{ - a\sin \theta }} = \frac{b}{a}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation x = sin t, y = cos 2t, without eliminating the parameter, find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{/tex}
question_html: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation x = sin t, y = cos 2t, without eliminating the parameter, find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{/tex}
solution_text: Given: x = sin t and y = cos 2t {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \cos t{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = - \sin 2t\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {2t} \right) = - 2\sin 2t{/tex} Now {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{ - 2\sin 2t}}{{\cos t}} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 2 \times 2\sin t\cos t}}{{\cos t}} = - 4\sin t{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: x = sin t and y = cos 2t

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \cos t{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = - \sin 2t\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {2t} \right) = - 2\sin 2t{/tex}

Now {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{ - 2\sin 2t}}{{\cos t}} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 2 \times 2\sin t\cos t}}{{\cos t}} = - 4\sin t{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation x = 4t, {tex}y = \frac{4}{t}{/tex} , without eliminating the parameter, find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{/tex}
question_html: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation x = 4t, {tex}y = \frac{4}{t}{/tex}, without eliminating the parameter, find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{/tex}
solution_text: Given: x = 4t and {tex}y = \frac{4}{t}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {4t} \right) = 4\frac{d}{{dt}}t = 4{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {\frac{4}{t}} \right) = \frac{{t\frac{d}{{dt}}4 - 4\frac{d}{{dt}}t}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{{t \times 0 - 4 \times 1}}{{{t^2}}} = - \frac{4}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} Now {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{ - \frac{4}{{{t^2}}}}}{4} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: x = 4t and {tex}y = \frac{4}{t}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {4t} \right) = 4\frac{d}{{dt}}t = 4{/tex}

and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {\frac{4}{t}} \right) = \frac{{t\frac{d}{{dt}}4 - 4\frac{d}{{dt}}t}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{{t \times 0 - 4 \times 1}}{{{t^2}}} = - \frac{4}{{{t^2}}}{/tex}

Now {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{ - \frac{4}{{{t^2}}}}}{4} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation {tex}x = \cos \theta - \cos 2\theta ,y = \sin \theta - \sin 2\theta {/tex} , without eliminating the parameter, find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{/tex}
question_html: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation {tex}x = \cos \theta - \cos 2\theta ,y = \sin \theta - \sin 2\theta {/tex}, without eliminating the parameter, find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{/tex}
solution_text: Given: {tex}x = \cos \theta - \cos 2\theta {/tex} and {tex}y = \sin \theta - \sin 2\theta{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\cos \theta - \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\cos 2\theta {/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\sin \theta - \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\sin 2\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = - \sin \theta - \left( { - \sin 2\theta } \right)\frac{d}{{d\theta }}2\theta{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = \cos \theta - \cos 2\theta \frac{d}{{d\theta }}2\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = - \sin \theta + \left( {\sin 2\theta } \right)2{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = \cos \theta - \cos 2\theta \times 2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = 2\sin 2\theta - \sin \theta {/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = \cos \theta - 2\cos 2\theta {/tex} Now {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/d\theta }}{{dx/d\theta }} = \frac{{\cos \theta - 2\cos 2\theta }}{{2\sin 2\theta - \sin \theta }}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}x = \cos \theta - \cos 2\theta {/tex} and {tex}y = \sin \theta - \sin 2\theta{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\cos \theta - \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\cos 2\theta {/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\sin \theta - \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\sin 2\theta{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = - \sin \theta - \left( { - \sin 2\theta } \right)\frac{d}{{d\theta }}2\theta{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = \cos \theta - \cos 2\theta \frac{d}{{d\theta }}2\theta{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = - \sin \theta + \left( {\sin 2\theta } \right)2{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = \cos \theta - \cos 2\theta \times 2{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = 2\sin 2\theta - \sin \theta {/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = \cos \theta - 2\cos 2\theta {/tex}

Now {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/d\theta }}{{dx/d\theta }} = \frac{{\cos \theta - 2\cos 2\theta }}{{2\sin 2\theta - \sin \theta }}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation x = a ( {tex}\theta{/tex} – sin {tex}\theta{/tex} ), y = a (1 + cos {tex}\theta{/tex} ) without eliminating the parameter. Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} .
question_html: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation x = a ({tex}\theta{/tex} – sin {tex}\theta{/tex}), y = a (1 + cos {tex}\theta{/tex}) without eliminating the parameter. Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.
solution_text: Given functions are x = {tex}a(\theta - sin \theta){/tex} and y = a(1 + cos {tex}\theta{/tex} ) {tex}\frac{d x}{d \theta}=a \frac{d}{d \theta}(\theta-\sin \theta){/tex} {tex}\frac{d x}{d \theta}=a\left[\frac{d}{d \theta} \theta-\frac{d}{d \theta} \sin \theta\right]{/tex} {tex}\frac{d x}{d \theta}=a(1-\cos \theta){/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d \theta}=a \frac{d}{d \theta}(1+\cos \theta){/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d \theta}=a\left[\frac{d}{d \theta}(1)+\frac{d}{d \theta} \cos \theta\right]{/tex} {tex}\frac{d \mathrm{y}}{d \theta}=a(0-\sin \theta){/tex} = -a sin {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y / d \theta}{d x / d \theta}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-a \sin \theta}{a(1-\cos \theta)}=\frac{-\sin \theta}{1-\cos \theta}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{2 \sin \frac{\theta}{2} \cos \frac{\theta}{2}}{2 \sin ^{2} \frac{\theta}{2}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\cos \frac{\theta}{2}}{\sin \frac{\theta}{2}}{/tex} = {tex}-\cot \frac{\theta}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given functions are x = {tex}a(\theta - sin \theta){/tex} and y = a(1 + cos{tex}\theta{/tex})
{tex}\frac{d x}{d \theta}=a \frac{d}{d \theta}(\theta-\sin \theta){/tex}
{tex}\frac{d x}{d \theta}=a\left[\frac{d}{d \theta} \theta-\frac{d}{d \theta} \sin \theta\right]{/tex}
{tex}\frac{d x}{d \theta}=a(1-\cos \theta){/tex}
{tex}\frac{d y}{d \theta}=a \frac{d}{d \theta}(1+\cos \theta){/tex}
{tex}\frac{d y}{d \theta}=a\left[\frac{d}{d \theta}(1)+\frac{d}{d \theta} \cos \theta\right]{/tex}
{tex}\frac{d \mathrm{y}}{d \theta}=a(0-\sin \theta){/tex}
= -a sin{tex}\theta{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y / d \theta}{d x / d \theta}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-a \sin \theta}{a(1-\cos \theta)}=\frac{-\sin \theta}{1-\cos \theta}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{2 \sin \frac{\theta}{2} \cos \frac{\theta}{2}}{2 \sin ^{2} \frac{\theta}{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{\cos \frac{\theta}{2}}{\sin \frac{\theta}{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}-\cot \frac{\theta}{2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation {tex}x=\frac{\sin ^{3} t}{\sqrt{\cos 2 t}}, y=\frac{\cos ^{3} t}{\sqrt{\cos 2 t}}{/tex} , without eliminating the parameter, Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
question_html: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation {tex}x=\frac{\sin ^{3} t}{\sqrt{\cos 2 t}}, y=\frac{\cos ^{3} t}{\sqrt{\cos 2 t}}{/tex}, without eliminating the parameter, Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
solution_text: It is given that {tex}\mathrm{x}=\frac{\sin ^{3} \mathrm{t}}{\sqrt{\cos 2 \mathrm{t}}}, \mathrm{y}=\frac{\cos ^{3} \mathrm{t}}{\sqrt{\cos 2 \mathrm{t}}}{/tex} Then, we have {tex}\frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{d\left(\frac{\sin ^{3} t}{\sqrt{\cos 2 t}}\right)}{d t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\cos 2 t} \cdot \frac{d\left(\sin ^{3} t\right)}{d t}-\sin ^{3} t \cdot \frac{d \sqrt{\cos 2 t}}{d t}}{\cos 2 t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\cos 2 t} \cdot 3 \sin ^{2} t \cdot \frac{d(\sin t)}{d t}-\sin ^{3} t \times \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\cos 2 t}} \frac{d(\cos 2 t)}{d t}}{\cos 2 t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3 \sqrt{\cos 2 t} \cdot \sin ^{2} t \cdot \cos t-\frac{\sin ^{3} t}{2 \sqrt{\cos 2 t}} \cdot(-2 \sin 2 t)}{\cos 2 t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3 \cos 2 t \cdot \sin ^{2} t \cdot \cos t+\sin ^{3} t \sin 2 t}{\cos 2 t \sqrt{\cos 2 t}}{/tex} .........(i) {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}=\frac{d\left(\frac{\cos ^{3} t}{\sqrt{\cos 2 t}}\right)}{d t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\cos 2 t} \cdot \frac{d\left(\cos ^{3} t\right)}{d t}-\cos ^{3} t \cdot \frac{d \sqrt{\cos 2 t}}{d t}}{\cos 2 t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\cos 2 t} \cdot 3 \cos ^{2} t \cdot \frac{d(\cos t)}{d t}-\cos ^{3} t \times \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\cos 2 t}} \frac{d(\cos 2 t)}{d t}}{\cos 2 t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3 \sqrt{\cos 2 t} \cos ^{2} t .(-\sin t)-\frac{\cos ^{3} t}{2 \sqrt{\cos 2 t}} \cdot(-2 \sin 2 t)}{\cos 2 t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-3 \cos 2 t \cdot \cos ^{2} t \cdot \sin t+\cos ^{3} t \sin 2 t}{\cos 2 t \sqrt{\cos 2 t}}{/tex} .........(ii) Therefore, from equation (i) and (ii), we get {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dt}}}{\frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{dt}}}=\frac{-3 \cos 2 \mathrm{t} \cdot \cos ^{2} \mathrm{t} \cdot \operatorname{sint}+\cos ^{3} \mathrm{t} \sin 2 \mathrm{t}}{3 \cos 2 \mathrm{t} \cdot \sin ^{2} \mathrm{t} \cdot \operatorname{cost}+\sin ^{3} \mathrm{tsin} 2 \mathrm{t}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-3 \cos 2 t \cdot \cos ^{2} t \cdot \sin t+\cos ^{3} t(2 \sin t \cos t)}{3 \cos 2 t \cdot \sin ^{2} t \cdot \cos t+\sin ^{3} t(2 \sin t \cos t)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sin t \cos t\left[-3 \cos 2 t \cdot \cos t+2 \cos ^{3} t\right]}{\sin t \cos t\left[3 \cos 2 t \cdot \sin t+2 \sin ^{3} t\right]}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\left[-3\left(2 \cos ^{2} t-1\right) \cos t+2 \cos ^{3} t\right]}{\left[3\left(1-2 \sin ^{2} t\right) \sin t+2 \sin ^{2} t\right]}{/tex} , [Since, cos 2t = (2 cos 2 t - 1) and also cos 2t = (1 - 2 sin 2 t)] = {tex}\frac{-4 \cos ^{3} t+3 \cos t}{3 \sin t-4 \sin ^{3} t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-\cos 3 t}{\sin 3 t}{/tex} [Since, cos 3t = 4 cos 3 t - 3 cos t, sin 3t = 3 sin t - 4 sin 3 t] = -cot 3t Hence, the value of {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} is -cot 3t
solution_html:
It is given that
{tex}\mathrm{x}=\frac{\sin ^{3} \mathrm{t}}{\sqrt{\cos 2 \mathrm{t}}}, \mathrm{y}=\frac{\cos ^{3} \mathrm{t}}{\sqrt{\cos 2 \mathrm{t}}}{/tex} 
Then, we have
{tex}\frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{d\left(\frac{\sin ^{3} t}{\sqrt{\cos 2 t}}\right)}{d t}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\cos 2 t} \cdot \frac{d\left(\sin ^{3} t\right)}{d t}-\sin ^{3} t \cdot \frac{d \sqrt{\cos 2 t}}{d t}}{\cos 2 t}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\cos 2 t} \cdot 3 \sin ^{2} t \cdot \frac{d(\sin t)}{d t}-\sin ^{3} t \times \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\cos 2 t}} \frac{d(\cos 2 t)}{d t}}{\cos 2 t}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{3 \sqrt{\cos 2 t} \cdot \sin ^{2} t \cdot \cos t-\frac{\sin ^{3} t}{2 \sqrt{\cos 2 t}} \cdot(-2 \sin 2 t)}{\cos 2 t}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{3 \cos 2 t \cdot \sin ^{2} t \cdot \cos t+\sin ^{3} t \sin 2 t}{\cos 2 t \sqrt{\cos 2 t}}{/tex} .........(i)
{tex}\frac{d y}{d t}=\frac{d\left(\frac{\cos ^{3} t}{\sqrt{\cos 2 t}}\right)}{d t}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\cos 2 t} \cdot \frac{d\left(\cos ^{3} t\right)}{d t}-\cos ^{3} t \cdot \frac{d \sqrt{\cos 2 t}}{d t}}{\cos 2 t}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\cos 2 t} \cdot 3 \cos ^{2} t \cdot \frac{d(\cos t)}{d t}-\cos ^{3} t \times \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\cos 2 t}} \frac{d(\cos 2 t)}{d t}}{\cos 2 t}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{3 \sqrt{\cos 2 t} \cos ^{2} t .(-\sin t)-\frac{\cos ^{3} t}{2 \sqrt{\cos 2 t}} \cdot(-2 \sin 2 t)}{\cos 2 t}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{-3 \cos 2 t \cdot \cos ^{2} t \cdot \sin t+\cos ^{3} t \sin 2 t}{\cos 2 t \sqrt{\cos 2 t}}{/tex} .........(ii)
Therefore, from equation (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dt}}}{\frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{dt}}}=\frac{-3 \cos 2 \mathrm{t} \cdot \cos ^{2} \mathrm{t} \cdot \operatorname{sint}+\cos ^{3} \mathrm{t} \sin 2 \mathrm{t}}{3 \cos 2 \mathrm{t} \cdot \sin ^{2} \mathrm{t} \cdot \operatorname{cost}+\sin ^{3} \mathrm{tsin} 2 \mathrm{t}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{-3 \cos 2 t \cdot \cos ^{2} t \cdot \sin t+\cos ^{3} t(2 \sin t \cos t)}{3 \cos 2 t \cdot \sin ^{2} t \cdot \cos t+\sin ^{3} t(2 \sin t \cos t)}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\sin t \cos t\left[-3 \cos 2 t \cdot \cos t+2 \cos ^{3} t\right]}{\sin t \cos t\left[3 \cos 2 t \cdot \sin t+2 \sin ^{3} t\right]}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\left[-3\left(2 \cos ^{2} t-1\right) \cos t+2 \cos ^{3} t\right]}{\left[3\left(1-2 \sin ^{2} t\right) \sin t+2 \sin ^{2} t\right]}{/tex} , [Since, cos 2t = (2 cos2 t - 1) and also cos 2t = (1 - 2 sin2 t)]
= {tex}\frac{-4 \cos ^{3} t+3 \cos t}{3 \sin t-4 \sin ^{3} t}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{-\cos 3 t}{\sin 3 t}{/tex} [Since, cos 3t = 4 cos3 t - 3 cos t, sin 3t = 3 sin t - 4 sin3 t]
= -cot 3t
Hence, the value of {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} is -cot 3t

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation {tex}x = a\left( {\cos t + \log \tan \frac{t}{2}} \right),{/tex} y = a sin t, without eliminating the parameter, find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} .
question_html: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation {tex}x = a\left( {\cos t + \log \tan \frac{t}{2}} \right),{/tex} y = a sin t, without eliminating the parameter, find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}.
solution_text: Given: {tex}x = a\left( {\cos t + \log \tan \frac{t}{2}} \right){/tex} and y = a sin t {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = a\left[ { - \sin t + \frac{1}{{\tan \frac{t}{2}}}.\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {\tan \frac{t}{2}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}= a\left[ { - \sin t + \frac{1}{{\tan \frac{t}{2}}}.{{\sec }^2}\frac{t}{2}.\frac{1}{2}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= a\left[ { - \sin t + \frac{{\cos \frac{t}{2}}}{{\sin \frac{t}{2}}}.\frac{1}{{{{\cos }^2}\frac{t}{2}}}.\frac{1}{2}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= a\left[ { - \sin t + \frac{1}{{2\sin \frac{t}{2}\cos \frac{t}{2}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= a\left[ { - \sin t + \frac{1}{{\sin t}}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = a\left( {\frac{{1 - {{\sin }^2}t}}{{\sin t}}} \right) = \frac{{a{{\cos }^2}t}}{{\sin t}}{/tex} And {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = a\cos t{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{a\cos t}}{{\left( {\frac{{a{{\cos }^2}t}}{{\sin t}}} \right)}} = \frac{{\sin t}}{{\cos t}} = \tan t{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}x = a\left( {\cos t + \log \tan \frac{t}{2}} \right){/tex} and y = a sin t
{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = a\left[ { - \sin t + \frac{1}{{\tan \frac{t}{2}}}.\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {\tan \frac{t}{2}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= a\left[ { - \sin t + \frac{1}{{\tan \frac{t}{2}}}.{{\sec }^2}\frac{t}{2}.\frac{1}{2}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= a\left[ { - \sin t + \frac{{\cos \frac{t}{2}}}{{\sin \frac{t}{2}}}.\frac{1}{{{{\cos }^2}\frac{t}{2}}}.\frac{1}{2}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= a\left[ { - \sin t + \frac{1}{{2\sin \frac{t}{2}\cos \frac{t}{2}}}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= a\left[ { - \sin t + \frac{1}{{\sin t}}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = a\left( {\frac{{1 - {{\sin }^2}t}}{{\sin t}}} \right) = \frac{{a{{\cos }^2}t}}{{\sin t}}{/tex}
And {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = a\cos t{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{a\cos t}}{{\left( {\frac{{a{{\cos }^2}t}}{{\sin t}}} \right)}} = \frac{{\sin t}}{{\cos t}} = \tan t{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation {tex}x = a\sec \theta,{/tex} {tex}y = b\tan \theta {/tex} , without eliminating the parameter, find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} .
question_html: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation {tex}x = a\sec \theta,{/tex} {tex}y = b\tan \theta {/tex}, without eliminating the parameter, find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}.
solution_text: Given: {tex}x = a\sec \theta{/tex} and {tex}y = b\tan \theta {/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = a\sec \theta \tan \theta{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = b{\sec ^2}\theta{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/d\theta }}{{dx/d\theta }} = \frac{{b{{\sec }^2}\theta }}{{a\sec \theta \tan \theta }}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{b\sec \theta }}{{a\tan \theta }}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{b.\frac{1}{{\cos \theta }}}}{{a.\frac{{\sin \theta }}{{\cos \theta }}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{b}{{a\sin \theta }}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{b}{a}\cos ec\theta{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}x = a\sec \theta{/tex} and {tex}y = b\tan \theta {/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = a\sec \theta \tan \theta{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = b{\sec ^2}\theta{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/d\theta }}{{dx/d\theta }} = \frac{{b{{\sec }^2}\theta }}{{a\sec \theta \tan \theta }}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{b\sec \theta }}{{a\tan \theta }}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{b.\frac{1}{{\cos \theta }}}}{{a.\frac{{\sin \theta }}{{\cos \theta }}}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{b}{{a\sin \theta }}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{b}{a}\cos ec\theta{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation {tex}x = a\left( {\cos \theta + \theta \sin \theta } \right),{/tex} {tex}y = a\left( {\sin \theta - \theta \cos \theta } \right){/tex} , without eliminating the parameter, find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} .
question_html: If x and y are connected parametrically by the equation {tex}x = a\left( {\cos \theta + \theta \sin \theta } \right),{/tex} {tex}y = a\left( {\sin \theta - \theta \cos \theta } \right){/tex}, without eliminating the parameter, find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}.
solution_text: Given: {tex}x = a\left( {\cos \theta + \theta \sin \theta } \right){/tex} and {tex}y = a\left( {\sin \theta - \theta \cos \theta } \right){/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = a\left( { - \sin \theta + \theta \cos \theta + \sin \theta .1} \right){/tex} {tex} = a\theta \cos \theta{/tex} And {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = a\left[ {\cos \theta - \left\{ {\theta \left( { - \sin \theta } \right) + \cos \theta .1} \right\}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= a\left[ {\cos \theta + \theta \sin \theta - \cos \theta } \right] = a\theta \sin \theta{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/d\theta }}{{dx/d\theta }} = \frac{{a\theta \sin \theta }}{{a\theta \cos \theta }} = \tan \theta{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}x = a\left( {\cos \theta + \theta \sin \theta } \right){/tex} and {tex}y = a\left( {\sin \theta - \theta \cos \theta } \right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = a\left( { - \sin \theta + \theta \cos \theta + \sin \theta .1} \right){/tex}
{tex} = a\theta \cos \theta{/tex}
And {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = a\left[ {\cos \theta - \left\{ {\theta \left( { - \sin \theta } \right) + \cos \theta .1} \right\}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= a\left[ {\cos \theta + \theta \sin \theta - \cos \theta } \right] = a\theta \sin \theta{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/d\theta }}{{dx/d\theta }} = \frac{{a\theta \sin \theta }}{{a\theta \cos \theta }} = \tan \theta{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x = {tex} \sqrt { a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t} }{/tex} , y = {tex} \sqrt { a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t}}{/tex} , show that {tex} \frac { d y } { d x } = -\frac { y } { x }{/tex} .
question_html: If x = {tex} \sqrt { a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t} }{/tex}, y = {tex} \sqrt { a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t}}{/tex}, show that {tex} \frac { d y } { d x } = -\frac { y } { x }{/tex}.
solution_text: According to the question, x = {tex} \sqrt { a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t} }{/tex} and y = {tex} \sqrt { a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t}}{/tex} Consider, {tex}x= \left( a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { 1 / 2 }{/tex} Differentiating both sides w.r.t x, {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d x } { d t } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { - 1 / 2 } \frac { d } { d t } \left( a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \right){/tex} [ Using chain rule of derivative] {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \mathrm { sin } ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { - 1 / 2 } a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \log a \frac { d } { d t } \left( \sin ^ { - 1 } t \right){/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { - 1 / 2 } a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \log a \cdot \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } }{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { 1 / 2 } \log a \cdot \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac { d x } { d t } = \frac { \frac { 1 } { 2 } \sqrt { a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } } \cdot \log a } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } }{/tex} ..........(i) Consider , {tex}y= \left( a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { 1 / 2 }{/tex} Differentiating both sides w.r.t x, {tex} \frac { d y } { d t } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { - 1 / 2 } \frac { d } { d t } \left( a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } \right){/tex} [Using chain rule of derivative] {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { - 1 / 2 } a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } \log a \frac { d } { d t } \left( \cos ^ { - 1 } t \right){/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { 1 / 2 } \log a \cdot \frac { ( - 1 ) } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac { d y } { d t } = \frac { - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \sqrt { a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } } \cdot \log a } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } }{/tex} ..........(ii) Dividing Eq.(ii) by Eq.(i), {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dt}}} \right)}}{{\left( {\frac{{dx}}{{dt}}} \right)}} = \frac{{\left( {\frac{{ - \frac{1}{2}\sqrt {{a^{{{\cos }^{ - 1}}t}}} \log a}}{{\sqrt {1 - {t^2}} }}} \right)}}{{\left( {\frac{{\frac{1}{2}\sqrt {{a^{{{\sin }^{ - 1}}t}}\log a} }}{{\sqrt {1 - {t^2}} }}} \right)}}{/tex} {tex} = - \frac { \sqrt { a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } } } { \sqrt { a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } } } = - \frac { y } { x }{/tex} Hence proved
solution_html:
According to the question,  x = {tex} \sqrt { a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t} }{/tex} and y = {tex} \sqrt { a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t}}{/tex}
Consider,  {tex}x= \left( a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { 1 / 2 }{/tex}
Differentiating both sides w.r.t x, 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d x } { d t } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { - 1 / 2 } \frac { d } { d t } \left( a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \right){/tex}[ Using chain rule of derivative]
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \mathrm { sin } ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { - 1 / 2 } a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \log a \frac { d } { d t } \left( \sin ^ { - 1 } t \right){/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { - 1 / 2 } a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \log a \cdot \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } }{/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { 1 / 2 } \log a \cdot \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } }{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac { d x } { d t } = \frac { \frac { 1 } { 2 } \sqrt { a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } } \cdot \log a } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } }{/tex}..........(i)
Consider , {tex}y= \left( a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { 1 / 2 }{/tex}
Differentiating both sides w.r.t x, 
{tex} \frac { d y } { d t } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { - 1 / 2 } \frac { d } { d t } \left( a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } \right){/tex} [Using chain rule of derivative]
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { - 1 / 2 } a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } \log a \frac { d } { d t } \left( \cos ^ { - 1 } t \right){/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left( a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } \right) ^ { 1 / 2 } \log a \cdot \frac { ( - 1 ) } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } }{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac { d y } { d t } = \frac { - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \sqrt { a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } } \cdot \log a } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } }{/tex}..........(ii)
Dividing Eq.(ii) by Eq.(i), 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dt}}} \right)}}{{\left( {\frac{{dx}}{{dt}}} \right)}} = \frac{{\left( {\frac{{ - \frac{1}{2}\sqrt {{a^{{{\cos }^{ - 1}}t}}} \log a}}{{\sqrt {1 - {t^2}} }}} \right)}}{{\left( {\frac{{\frac{1}{2}\sqrt {{a^{{{\sin }^{ - 1}}t}}\log a} }}{{\sqrt {1 - {t^2}} }}} \right)}}{/tex}
{tex} = - \frac { \sqrt { a ^ { \cos ^ { - 1 } t } } } { \sqrt { a ^ { \sin ^ { - 1 } t } } } = - \frac { y } { x }{/tex}
Hence proved

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the second-order derivatives of the function x 2 + 3x + 2
question_html: Find the second-order derivatives of the function x2 + 3x + 2
solution_text: Let us take y = x 2 + 3x + 2 Now, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d\left(x^{2}\right)}{d x}+\frac{d(3 x)}{d x}+\frac{d(2)}{d x}{/tex} = 2x + 3 Therefore, {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{d(2 x+3)}{d x}=\frac{d(2 x)}{d x}+\frac{d(3)}{d x}{/tex} = 2 + 0 = 2
solution_html:
Let us take y = x2 + 3x + 2
Now, 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d\left(x^{2}\right)}{d x}+\frac{d(3 x)}{d x}+\frac{d(2)}{d x}{/tex}
= 2x + 3
Therefore,
{tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{d(2 x+3)}{d x}=\frac{d(2 x)}{d x}+\frac{d(3)}{d x}{/tex} 
= 2 + 0
= 2

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the second-order derivatives of the function x 20
question_html: Find the second-order derivatives of the function x20
solution_text: Let us take y = x 20 Now, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}(x^{20}){/tex} = 20 x 19 Therefore, {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(20 x^{19}\right)=20 \frac{d}{d x}{\left(x^{19}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}20 \times 19 \times x^{18}{/tex} = 380 x 18
solution_html:
Let us take y = x20 
Now,
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}(x^{20}){/tex} 
= 20 x19 
Therefore,
{tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(20 x^{19}\right)=20 \frac{d}{d x}{\left(x^{19}\right)}{/tex}  = {tex}20 \times 19 \times x^{18}{/tex} 
= 380 x18

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the second-order derivative of the function x.cos x
question_html: Find the second-order derivative of the function x.cos x
solution_text: Let y = x.cos x {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = x\frac{d}{{dx}}\cos x + \cos x\frac{d}{{dx}}x = - x\sin x + \cos x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right) = - \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {x\sin x} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\cos x{/tex} {tex}= - \left[ {x\frac{d}{{dx}}\sin x + \sin x\frac{d}{{dx}}x} \right] - \sin x{/tex} = -(x cos x + sin x) - sin x = - x cos x - sin x - sin x = -x cos x - 2 sin x = -(x cos x + 2 sin x). Which is the required solution.
solution_html:
Let y = x.cos x
{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = x\frac{d}{{dx}}\cos x + \cos x\frac{d}{{dx}}x = - x\sin x + \cos x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right) = - \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {x\sin x} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\cos x{/tex}
{tex}= - \left[ {x\frac{d}{{dx}}\sin x + \sin x\frac{d}{{dx}}x} \right] - \sin x{/tex}
= -(x cos x + sin x) - sin x
= - x cos x - sin x - sin x
= -x cos x - 2 sin x
= -(x cos x + 2 sin x).
Which is the required solution.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the second-order derivative of the function log x
question_html: Find the second-order derivative of the function log x
solution_text: Let y = log x Now, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}(\log x)=\frac{1}{x}{/tex} Therefore, {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=\left(-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right){/tex}
solution_html:
Let   y = log x 
Now,
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}(\log x)=\frac{1}{x}{/tex} 
Therefore,
{tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=\left(-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the second-order derivative of the function x 3 log x
question_html: Find the second-order derivative of the function x3 log x
solution_text: Let y = x 3 log x {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = {x^3}\frac{d}{{dx}}\log x + \log x\frac{d}{{dx}}{x^3}{/tex} {tex}= {x^3}.\frac{1}{x} + \log x\left( {3{x^2}} \right){/tex} = x 2 + 3x 2 log x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2} + 3{x^2}\log x} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{d}{{dx}}{x^2} + 3\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2}\log x} \right){/tex} {tex}= 2x + 3\left[ {{x^2}\frac{d}{{dx}}\log x + \log x\frac{d}{{dx}}{x^2}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= 2x + 3\left( {{x^2}.\frac{1}{x} + \left( {\log x} \right)2x} \right){/tex} = 2x + 3(x + 2x log x) = 2x + 3x + 6x log x = 5x + 6x log x = x(5 + 6 log x)
solution_html:
Let y = x3log x

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = {x^3}\frac{d}{{dx}}\log x + \log x\frac{d}{{dx}}{x^3}{/tex}

{tex}= {x^3}.\frac{1}{x} + \log x\left( {3{x^2}} \right){/tex}

= x2 + 3x2log x

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2} + 3{x^2}\log x} \right){/tex}

{tex}= \frac{d}{{dx}}{x^2} + 3\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2}\log x} \right){/tex}

{tex}= 2x + 3\left[ {{x^2}\frac{d}{{dx}}\log x + \log x\frac{d}{{dx}}{x^2}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= 2x + 3\left( {{x^2}.\frac{1}{x} + \left( {\log x} \right)2x} \right){/tex}

= 2x + 3(x + 2x log x)

= 2x + 3x + 6x log x

= 5x + 6x log x

= x(5 + 6 log x)

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the second-order derivative of the function e x sin 5x
question_html: Find the second-order derivative of the function exsin 5x
solution_text: Let y = e x sin 5x {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = {e^x}\frac{d}{{dx}}\sin 5x + \sin 5x\frac{d}{{dx}}{e^x}{/tex} {tex}= {e^x}\cos 5x\frac{d}{{dx}}5x + \sin 5x.{e^x} {/tex} {tex}= {e^x}\cos 5x \times 5 + {e^x}\sin 5x{/tex} = e x (5 cos 5x + sin 5x) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = {e^x}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {5\cos 5x + \sin 5x} \right) + \left( {5\cos 5x + \sin 5x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}{e^x}{/tex} {tex}= {e^x}\left[ {5\left( { - \sin 5x} \right) \times 5 + \left( {\cos 5x} \right) \times 5} \right] {/tex} + (5 cos 5x + sin 5x)e x = e x (-25 sin 5x + 5 cos 5x + 5 cos 5x + sin 5x) = e x (10 cos 5x - 24 sin 5x) = 2e x (5 cos 5x - 12 sin 5x)
solution_html:
Let y = exsin 5x

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = {e^x}\frac{d}{{dx}}\sin 5x + \sin 5x\frac{d}{{dx}}{e^x}{/tex}

{tex}= {e^x}\cos 5x\frac{d}{{dx}}5x + \sin 5x.{e^x} {/tex} {tex}= {e^x}\cos 5x \times 5 + {e^x}\sin 5x{/tex}

= ex(5 cos 5x + sin 5x)

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = {e^x}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {5\cos 5x + \sin 5x} \right) + \left( {5\cos 5x + \sin 5x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}{e^x}{/tex}

{tex}= {e^x}\left[ {5\left( { - \sin 5x} \right) \times 5 + \left( {\cos 5x} \right) \times 5} \right] {/tex} + (5 cos 5x + sin 5x)ex

= ex(-25 sin 5x + 5 cos 5x + 5 cos 5x + sin 5x)

= ex(10 cos 5x - 24 sin 5x)

= 2ex(5 cos 5x - 12 sin 5x)

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the second-order derivatives of the function e 6x cos 3x
question_html: Find the second-order derivatives of the function e6x cos 3x
solution_text: Let y = e 6x cos3x {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = {e^{6x}}.\frac{d}{{dx}}\cos 3x + \cos 3x\frac{d}{{dx}}{e^{6x}}{/tex} {tex}= {e^{6x}}\left( { - \sin 3x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {3x} \right) + \cos 3x.{e^{6x}}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {6x} \right){/tex} {tex}= -{e^{6x}}\sin 3x \times 3 + \cos 3x.{e^{6x}} \times 6{/tex} = e 6x (-3 sin 3x + 6 cos 3x) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = {e^{6x}}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( { - 3\sin 3x + 6\cos 3x} \right) {/tex} {tex}+ \left( { - 3\sin 3x + 6\cos 3x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}{e^{6x}}{/tex} {tex}= {e^{6x}}\left( { - 3\cos 3x \times 3 - 6\sin 3x \times 3} \right) {/tex} {tex}+ \left( { - 3\sin 3x + 6\cos 3x} \right){e^{6x}} \times 6{/tex} = e 6x (-9 cos 3x - 18 sin 3x - 18 sin 3x + 36 cos 3x) = e 6x (27cos 3x - 36sin3x) = 9e 6x (3 cos 3x - 4 sin 3x)
solution_html:
Let y = e6xcos3x

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = {e^{6x}}.\frac{d}{{dx}}\cos 3x + \cos 3x\frac{d}{{dx}}{e^{6x}}{/tex}

{tex}= {e^{6x}}\left( { - \sin 3x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {3x} \right) + \cos 3x.{e^{6x}}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {6x} \right){/tex}

{tex}= -{e^{6x}}\sin 3x \times 3 + \cos 3x.{e^{6x}} \times 6{/tex}

= e6x(-3 sin 3x + 6 cos 3x)

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = {e^{6x}}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( { - 3\sin 3x + 6\cos 3x} \right) {/tex} {tex}+ \left( { - 3\sin 3x + 6\cos 3x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}{e^{6x}}{/tex}

{tex}= {e^{6x}}\left( { - 3\cos 3x \times 3 - 6\sin 3x \times 3} \right) {/tex} {tex}+ \left( { - 3\sin 3x + 6\cos 3x} \right){e^{6x}} \times 6{/tex}

= e6x(-9 cos 3x - 18 sin 3x - 18 sin 3x + 36 cos 3x)

= e6x(27cos 3x - 36sin3x)

= 9e6x(3 cos 3x - 4 sin 3x)

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the second-order derivatives of the function tan -1 x
question_html: Find the second-order derivatives of the function tan-1x
solution_text: Let y = tan -1 x {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( 1 \right) - 1\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}}{{{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right) \times 0 - 2x}}{{{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}^2}}} = \frac{{ - 2x}}{{{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let y = tan-1x

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right){/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( 1 \right) - 1\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}}{{{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right) \times 0 - 2x}}{{{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}^2}}} = \frac{{ - 2x}}{{{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the second-order derivative of the function log(log x)
question_html: Find the second-order derivative of the function log(log x)
solution_text: Let y = log(log x) {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{{\log x}}\frac{d}{{dx}}\log x\left[ {\therefore \frac{d}{{dx}}\log f\left( x \right) = \frac{1}{{f\left( x \right)}}\frac{d}{{dx}}f\left( x \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{{\log x}}.\frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{{x\log x}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{\left( {x\log x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( 1 \right) - 1\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {x\log x} \right)}}{{{{\left( {x\log x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\left( {x\log x} \right)\left( 0 \right) - \left[ {x\frac{d}{{dx}}\log x + \log x\frac{d}{{dx}}x} \right]}}{{{{\left( {x\log x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - \left[ {x.\frac{1}{x} + \log x \times 1} \right]}}{{{{\left( {x\log x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - \left[ {1 + \log x} \right]}}{{{{\left( {x\log x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let y = log(log x)

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{{\log x}}\frac{d}{{dx}}\log x\left[ {\therefore \frac{d}{{dx}}\log f\left( x \right) = \frac{1}{{f\left( x \right)}}\frac{d}{{dx}}f\left( x \right)} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{1}{{\log x}}.\frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{{x\log x}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{\left( {x\log x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( 1 \right) - 1\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {x\log x} \right)}}{{{{\left( {x\log x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{\left( {x\log x} \right)\left( 0 \right) - \left[ {x\frac{d}{{dx}}\log x + \log x\frac{d}{{dx}}x} \right]}}{{{{\left( {x\log x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - \left[ {x.\frac{1}{x} + \log x \times 1} \right]}}{{{{\left( {x\log x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - \left[ {1 + \log x} \right]}}{{{{\left( {x\log x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the second-order derivative of the function sin(log x)
question_html: Find the second-order derivative of the function sin(log x)
solution_text: Let y = sin (log x) {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \cos \left( {\log x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\log x} \right){/tex} {tex}= \cos \left( {\log x} \right).\frac{1}{x} = \frac{{\cos \left( {\log x} \right)}}{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{x\frac{d}{{dx}}\cos \left( {\log x} \right) - \cos \left( {\log x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}x}}{{{x^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{x (-sinlogx)\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\log x} \right) - \cos \left( {\log x} \right) \times 1}}{{{x^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - x\sin \left( {\log x} \right)\frac{1}{x} - \cos \left( {\log x} \right)}}{{{x^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - \left[ {\sin \left( {\log x} \right) + \cos \left( {\log x } \right)} \right]}}{{{x^2}}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let y = sin (log x)

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \cos \left( {\log x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\log x} \right){/tex}

{tex}= \cos \left( {\log x} \right).\frac{1}{x} = \frac{{\cos \left( {\log x} \right)}}{x}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{x\frac{d}{{dx}}\cos \left( {\log x} \right) - \cos \left( {\log x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}x}}{{{x^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{x (-sinlogx)\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\log x} \right) - \cos \left( {\log x} \right) \times 1}}{{{x^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - x\sin \left( {\log x} \right)\frac{1}{x} - \cos \left( {\log x} \right)}}{{{x^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - \left[ {\sin \left( {\log x} \right) + \cos \left( {\log x } \right)} \right]}}{{{x^2}}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If y = 5 cos x - 3 sin x, prove that {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} + y = 0{/tex}
question_html: If y = 5 cos x - 3 sin x, prove that {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} + y = 0{/tex}
solution_text: Let y = 5 cos x - 3 sin x ....(i) {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - 5\sin x - 3\cos x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = - 5\cos x + 3\sin x{/tex} = -(5 cos x - 3 sin x) = -y [From eq. (i)] {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = - y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} + y = 0{/tex}
solution_html:
Let y = 5 cos x - 3 sin x ....(i)

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - 5\sin x - 3\cos x{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = - 5\cos x + 3\sin x{/tex}

= -(5 cos x - 3 sin x) = -y [From eq. (i)]

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = - y{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} + y = 0{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If y = cos -1 x. Find {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}{/tex} in terms of y alone.
question_html: If y = cos-1x. Find {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}{/tex} in terms of y alone.
