solution_text: Given: y = cos -1 x {tex}\Rightarrow x = \cos y{/tex} .....(i) {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sqrt {1 - {{\cos }^2}y} }}{/tex} [From eq. (i)] {tex} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sqrt {{{\sin }^2}y} }} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sin y}} = - {\text{cosec}}\,y{/tex} .....(ii) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = - \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\text cosec\,y} \right){/tex} {tex}= - \left[ { - \ cosec\ y\,\cot y\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right]{/tex} = cosecy cot y(-cosec y) [ using (ii)] = -cosec 2 y coty
solution_html:
Given: y = cos-1x

{tex}\Rightarrow x = \cos y{/tex} .....(i)

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sqrt {1 - {{\cos }^2}y} }}{/tex} [From eq. (i)]

{tex} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sqrt {{{\sin }^2}y} }} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sin y}} = - {\text{cosec}}\,y{/tex} .....(ii)

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = - \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\text cosec\,y} \right){/tex}

{tex}= - \left[ { - \ cosec\ y\,\cot y\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right]{/tex}

= cosecy cot y(-cosec y) [ using (ii)]

= -cosec2y coty

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If y = 3 cos (log x) + 4 sin (log x). Show that x 2 y 2 + xy 1 + y = 0
question_html: If y = 3 cos (log x) + 4 sin (log x). Show that x2y2 + xy1 + y = 0
solution_text: {tex}y = 3\cos (\log x) + 4\sin (\log x){/tex} Differentiating both side w.r.t. to x {tex}{y_1} = \frac{{ - 3\sin (\log x)}}{x} + \frac{{4\cos (\log x)}}{x}{/tex} {tex}x{y_1} = - 3\sin (\log x) + 4\cos (\log x){/tex} Again differentiating, {tex}x{y_2} + {y_1}.1 = \frac{{ - 3\cos (\log x)}}{x} - \frac{{4\sin (\log x)}}{x}{/tex} {tex}{x^2}{y_2} + x{y_1} = - 3\cos (\log x) - 4\sin (\log x){/tex} {tex}{x^2}{y_2} + x{y_1} = - y{/tex} {tex}{x^2}{y_2} + x{y_1} + y = 0{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}y = 3\cos (\log x) + 4\sin (\log x){/tex}

Differentiating both side w.r.t. to x

{tex}{y_1} = \frac{{ - 3\sin (\log x)}}{x} + \frac{{4\cos (\log x)}}{x}{/tex}

{tex}x{y_1} = - 3\sin (\log x) + 4\cos (\log x){/tex}

Again differentiating,

{tex}x{y_2} + {y_1}.1 = \frac{{ - 3\cos (\log x)}}{x} - \frac{{4\sin (\log x)}}{x}{/tex}

{tex}{x^2}{y_2} + x{y_1} = - 3\cos (\log x) - 4\sin (\log x){/tex}

{tex}{x^2}{y_2} + x{y_1} = - y{/tex}

 {tex}{x^2}{y_2} + x{y_1} + y = 0{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If y = Ae mx + Be nx , show that {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} - \left( {m + n} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + mny = 0{/tex}
question_html: If y = Aemx + Benx, show that {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} - \left( {m + n} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + mny = 0{/tex}
solution_text: Given: y = Ae mx + Be nx ....(i) To prove: {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} - \left( {m + n} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + mny = 0{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} =A{e^{mx}}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {mx} \right) + B{e^{nx}}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {nx} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \frac{d}{{dx}}{e^{f\left( x \right)}} = {e^{f\left( x \right)}}\frac{d}{{dx}}f\left( x \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = Am{e^{mx}} + Bn{e^{nx}}{/tex} ….(ii) Again {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{{d}x^2}} = Am{e^{mx}}.m + Bn{e^{nx}}.n{/tex} = Am 2 e mx + Bn 2 e nx ...(iii) Now, L.H.S.= {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} - \left( {m + n} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + mny{/tex} = Am 2 e mx + Bn 2 e nx - (m + n)(Ame mx + Bne nx ) + mn(Ae mx + Be nx ) = Am 2 e mx + Bn 2 e nx - Am 2 e mx - Bmne nx - Amne mx - Bn 2 e nx + Amne mx + Bmne nx = 0 = R.H.S. Hence proved.
solution_html:
Given: y = Aemx + Benx ....(i)

To prove: {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} - \left( {m + n} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + mny = 0{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} =A{e^{mx}}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {mx} \right) + B{e^{nx}}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {nx} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \frac{d}{{dx}}{e^{f\left( x \right)}} = {e^{f\left( x \right)}}\frac{d}{{dx}}f\left( x \right)} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = Am{e^{mx}} + Bn{e^{nx}}{/tex}….(ii)

Again {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{{d}x^2}} = Am{e^{mx}}.m + Bn{e^{nx}}.n{/tex}

= Am2emx + Bn2enx ...(iii)

Now, L.H.S.= {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} - \left( {m + n} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + mny{/tex}

= Am2emx + Bn2enx - (m + n)(Amemx + Bnenx) + mn(Aemx + Benx)

= Am2emx + Bn2enx - Am2emx - Bmnenx - Amnemx - Bn2enx + Amnemx + Bmnenx

= 0

= R.H.S. Hence proved.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If y = 500e 7x + 600e -7x show that {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = 49y{/tex} .
question_html: If y = 500e7x + 600e-7x show that {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = 49y{/tex}.
solution_text: Given: y = 500e 7x + 600e -7x ...(i) {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} {/tex} = 500e 7x (7) + 600e -7x (-7) = 500(7)e 7x - 600(7)e -7x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} {/tex} = 500(7)e 7x (7) - 600(7)e -7x (-7) = 500(49)e 7x + 600(49){e -7x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} {/tex} = 49[500e 7x + 600e -7x ] = 49y [From eq. (i)] {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = 49y{/tex} Hence proved.
solution_html:
Given: y = 500e7x + 600e-7x ...(i)

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} {/tex} = 500e7x(7) + 600e-7x(-7) = 500(7)e7x - 600(7)e-7x

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} {/tex} = 500(7)e7x(7) - 600(7)e-7x(-7) = 500(49)e7x + 600(49){e-7x

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} {/tex} = 49[500e7x + 600e-7x]

= 49y [From eq. (i)]

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = 49y{/tex} Hence proved.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If e y (x + 1) = 1, show that {tex}\frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } = \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 }{/tex}
question_html: If ey(x + 1) = 1, show that {tex}\frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } = \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 }{/tex}
solution_text: According to the question, {tex}e^y (x + 1) = 1{/tex} Taking log both sides, {tex}\Rightarrow log [e^y (x + 1) ]= log 1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow log e^y + log(x + 1) = log 1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}y + log(x + 1) = log1{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} log e y = y] differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, {tex}\Rightarrow\frac { d y } { d x } + \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } = 0{/tex} .........(i) Differentiating both sides w.r.t. 'x', {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \frac { 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \left( - \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 } = 0{/tex} [ From Equation(i)] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 } = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } = \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 }{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question, {tex}e^y (x + 1) = 1{/tex}
Taking log both sides, 
{tex}\Rightarrow log [e^y (x + 1) ]= log 1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow log e^y + log(x + 1) = log 1{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}y + log(x + 1) = log1{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} log ey = y]
 differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, 
{tex}\Rightarrow\frac { d y } { d x } + \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } = 0{/tex}.........(i)
Differentiating both sides w.r.t. 'x', 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \frac { 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \left( - \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 } = 0{/tex} [ From Equation(i)]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 } = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } = \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 }{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If y = (tan -1 x) 2 show that (x 2 + 1) 2 y 2 + 2x(x 2 + 1)y 1 = 2
question_html: If y = (tan-1x)2 show that (x2 + 1)2 y2 + 2x(x2 + 1)y1 = 2
solution_text: y = (tan -1 x) 2 (given) Differentiating both sides w.r.t to x {tex}{y_1} = 2{\tan ^{ - 1}}x.\frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}{/tex} (1 + x 2 )y 1 = 2tan -1 x Again differentiating both sides w.r.t to x {tex}\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right){y_2} + {y_1}\left( {2x} \right) = 2.\frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}{/tex} (1 + x 2 ) 2 y 2 + 2x(x 2 + 1)y 1 = 2
solution_html:
y = (tan-1x)2 (given)

Differentiating both sides w.r.t to x

{tex}{y_1} = 2{\tan ^{ - 1}}x.\frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}{/tex}

(1 + x2)y1 = 2tan-1x

Again differentiating both sides w.r.t to x

{tex}\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right){y_2} + {y_1}\left( {2x} \right) = 2.\frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}{/tex}

(1 + x2)2 y2 + 2x(x2 + 1)y1 = 2

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Differentiate the function {tex}{\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right)^9}{/tex} w.r.t to x.
question_html: Differentiate the function {tex}{\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right)^9}{/tex} w.r.t to x.
solution_text: Let {tex}y = {\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right)^9}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 9{\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right)^8}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \frac{d}{{dx}}{{\left\{ {f\left( x \right)} \right\}}^4} = n{{\left\{ {f\left( x \right)} \right\}}^{n - 1}}\frac{d}{{dx}}f\left( x \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 9{\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right)^8}\left[ {3\left( {2x} \right) - 9\left( 1 \right) + 0} \right] = 9{\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right)^8}\left[ {6x - 9} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 27{\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right)^8}\left[ {2x - 3} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}y = {\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right)^9}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 9{\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right)^8}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right){/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\because \frac{d}{{dx}}{{\left\{ {f\left( x \right)} \right\}}^4} = n{{\left\{ {f\left( x \right)} \right\}}^{n - 1}}\frac{d}{{dx}}f\left( x \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 9{\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right)^8}\left[ {3\left( {2x} \right) - 9\left( 1 \right) + 0} \right] = 9{\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right)^8}\left[ {6x - 9} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 27{\left( {3{x^2} - 9x + 5} \right)^8}\left[ {2x - 3} \right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Differentiate the function sin 3 x + cos 6 x, w.r.t to x.
question_html: Differentiate the function sin3 x + cos6 x,  w.r.t to x.
solution_text: Let y = sin 3 x + cos 6 x Differentiating both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{3} x\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(\cos ^{6} x\right){/tex} = {tex}3 \sin ^{2} x \times \frac{d}{d x}(\sin x)+6 \cos ^{5} x \times \frac{d}{d x}(\cos x){/tex} = {tex}3 \sin ^{2} x \times \cos x+6 \cos ^{5} x \times(-\sin x){/tex} {tex}[ \because~\left.\frac{d}{d x}(\sin x)=\cos x\ \& \frac{d}{d x}(\cos x)=-\sin x\right]{/tex} = {tex}3 \sin x \cos x\left(\sin x-2 \cos ^{4} x\right){/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=3 \sin x \cos x\left(\sin x-2 \cos ^{4} x\right){/tex}
solution_html:
Let y = sin3 x + cos6 x
Differentiating both sides with respect to x
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{3} x\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(\cos ^{6} x\right){/tex}   
= {tex}3 \sin ^{2} x \times \frac{d}{d x}(\sin x)+6 \cos ^{5} x \times \frac{d}{d x}(\cos x){/tex} 
= {tex}3 \sin ^{2} x \times \cos x+6 \cos ^{5} x \times(-\sin x){/tex}        {tex}[ \because~\left.\frac{d}{d x}(\sin x)=\cos x\ \& \frac{d}{d x}(\cos x)=-\sin x\right]{/tex}
 = {tex}3 \sin x \cos x\left(\sin x-2 \cos ^{4} x\right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=3 \sin x \cos x\left(\sin x-2 \cos ^{4} x\right){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Differentiate the function (5x) 3 cos 2x w.r.t to x.
question_html: Differentiate the function (5x)3 cos 2x w.r.t to x.
solution_text: Let y = (5x) 3 cos 2x Then, log y = log (5x) 3 cos 2x {tex}\Rightarrow \log y=3 \cos 2 x \times \log 5 x{/tex} Differentiating both sides with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{1}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}3\left[\log 5 x \times \frac{d}{d x}(\cos 2 x)+\cos 2 x \times \frac{d}{d x}(\log 5 x)\right]{/tex} ...[ {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{d}{d x}(u v)=u \times \frac{d v}{d x}+v \times \frac{d u}{d x}{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=3 \mathrm{y}\left[\log 5 \mathrm{x}(-2 \sin 2 \mathrm{x}) \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(2 \mathrm{x})+\cos 2 \mathrm{x} \times \frac{1}{5 \mathrm{x}} \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(5 \mathrm{x})\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=3 y\left[-2 \sin 2 x \log 5 x+\frac{\cos 2 x}{x}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=y\left[\frac{3 \cos 2 x}{x}-6 \sin 2 x \log 5 x\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=(5 x)^{3} \cos 2 x\left[\frac{3 \cos 2 x}{x}-6 \sin 2 x \log 5 x\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=(5 x)^{3} \cos 2 x\left[\frac{3 \cos 2 x}{x}-6 \sin 2 x \log 5 x\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let   y = (5x)3 cos 2x 
Then,  log y = log (5x)3 cos 2x 
{tex}\Rightarrow \log y=3 \cos 2 x \times \log 5 x{/tex} 
Differentiating both sides with respect to x, we get
{tex}\frac{1}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}3\left[\log 5 x \times \frac{d}{d x}(\cos 2 x)+\cos 2 x \times \frac{d}{d x}(\log 5 x)\right]{/tex}    ...[{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{d}{d x}(u v)=u \times \frac{d v}{d x}+v \times \frac{d u}{d x}{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=3 \mathrm{y}\left[\log 5 \mathrm{x}(-2 \sin 2 \mathrm{x}) \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(2 \mathrm{x})+\cos 2 \mathrm{x} \times \frac{1}{5 \mathrm{x}} \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(5 \mathrm{x})\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=3 y\left[-2 \sin 2 x \log 5 x+\frac{\cos 2 x}{x}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=y\left[\frac{3 \cos 2 x}{x}-6 \sin 2 x \log 5 x\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=(5 x)^{3} \cos 2 x\left[\frac{3 \cos 2 x}{x}-6 \sin 2 x \log 5 x\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=(5 x)^{3} \cos 2 x\left[\frac{3 \cos 2 x}{x}-6 \sin 2 x \log 5 x\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Differentiate the function {tex}sin ^{-1}({x\sqrt x})\ ,{0 \leq x \leq 1}{/tex} w.r.t. to x.
question_html: Differentiate the function {tex}sin ^{-1}({x\sqrt x})\ ,{0 \leq x \leq 1}{/tex} w.r.t. to x.
solution_text: Let {tex}y = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {x\sqrt x } \right) = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}} \right){/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - \left( {{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}} \right)^2} }}\frac{d}{{dx}}{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^3}} }}.\frac{3}{2}{x^{\frac{1}{2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{3\sqrt x }}{{2\sqrt {1 - {x^3}} }}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{3}{2}\sqrt {\frac{x}{{1 - {x^3}}}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}y = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {x\sqrt x } \right) = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}} \right){/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - \left( {{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}} \right)^2} }}\frac{d}{{dx}}{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^3}} }}.\frac{3}{2}{x^{\frac{1}{2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{3\sqrt x }}{{2\sqrt {1 - {x^3}} }}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{3}{2}\sqrt {\frac{x}{{1 - {x^3}}}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Differentiate the function {tex}\frac{\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}}{\sqrt{2 x+7}},-2<x<2{/tex} w.r.t to x.
question_html: Differentiate the function {tex}\frac{\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}}{\sqrt{2 x+7}},-2<x<2{/tex} w.r.t to x.
solution_text: Let y = {tex}\frac{\cos ^{-1} \frac x2}{\sqrt{2 x+7}},-2<x<2{/tex} Differentiating both sides with respect to x, we get Using Quotient rule {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sqrt{2 x+7} \frac{d}{d x}\left(\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right)-\left(\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right) \frac{d}{d x}(\sqrt{2 x+7})}{(\sqrt{2 x+7})^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{2 x+7}\left[\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)^{2}}} \times \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)\right]-\left(\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right) \times \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2 x+7}} \times \frac{d}{d x}(2 x+7)}{2 x+7}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sqrt{2 x+7} \times-\frac{1}{\sqrt{4-x^{2}}}-\left(\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right) \times \frac{2}{2 \sqrt{2 x+7}}}{2 x+7}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{\sqrt{2 x+7}}{\sqrt{4-x^{2}} \times(2 x+7)}-\frac{\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}}{(\sqrt{2 x+7})(2 x+7)}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{4-x^{2}} \times \sqrt{2 x+7}}+\frac{\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}}{(2 x+7)^{\frac{3}{2}}}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let y = {tex}\frac{\cos ^{-1} \frac x2}{\sqrt{2 x+7}},-2<x<2{/tex} 
Differentiating both sides with respect to x, we get
Using Quotient rule
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sqrt{2 x+7} \frac{d}{d x}\left(\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right)-\left(\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right) \frac{d}{d x}(\sqrt{2 x+7})}{(\sqrt{2 x+7})^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{2 x+7}\left[\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)^{2}}} \times \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)\right]-\left(\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right) \times \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2 x+7}} \times \frac{d}{d x}(2 x+7)}{2 x+7}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sqrt{2 x+7} \times-\frac{1}{\sqrt{4-x^{2}}}-\left(\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right) \times \frac{2}{2 \sqrt{2 x+7}}}{2 x+7}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{\sqrt{2 x+7}}{\sqrt{4-x^{2}} \times(2 x+7)}-\frac{\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}}{(\sqrt{2 x+7})(2 x+7)}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{4-x^{2}} \times \sqrt{2 x+7}}+\frac{\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}}{(2 x+7)^{\frac{3}{2}}}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Differentiate the function {tex}\cot ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{1+\sin x}+\sqrt{1-\sin x}}{\sqrt{1+\sin x}-\sqrt{1-\sin x}}\right], 0<x<\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} w.r.t. x.
question_html: Differentiate the function {tex}\cot ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{1+\sin x}+\sqrt{1-\sin x}}{\sqrt{1+\sin x}-\sqrt{1-\sin x}}\right], 0<x<\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} w.r.t. x.
solution_text: {tex}y = {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\sqrt {{{\left( {\cos \frac{x}{2} + \sin \frac{x}{2}} \right)}^2}} + \sqrt {{{\left( {\cos \frac{x}{2} - \sin \frac{x}{2}} \right)}^2}} }}{{\sqrt {{{\left( {\cos \frac{x}{2} + \sin \frac{x}{2}} \right)}^2}} - \sqrt {{{\left( {\cos \frac{x}{2} - \sin \frac{x}{2}} \right)}^2}} }}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because\sqrt {1 \pm \sin x} = \sqrt {{{\left( {\cos \frac{x}{2} \pm \sin \frac{x}{2}} \right)}^2}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= {\cot ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\cos \frac{x}{2} + \sin \frac{x}{2} + \cos \frac{x}{2} - \sin \frac{x}{2}}}{{\cos \frac{x}{2} + \sin \frac{x}{2} - \cos \frac{x}{2} + \sin \frac{x}{2}}}{/tex} {tex} = {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2\cos \frac{x}{2}}}{{2\sin \frac{x}{2}}}} \right){/tex} {tex} = {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cot \frac{x}{2}} \right){/tex} {tex} = \frac{x}{2}{/tex} {tex}y = \frac{x}{2}{/tex} {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}y = {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{\sqrt {{{\left( {\cos \frac{x}{2} + \sin \frac{x}{2}} \right)}^2}} + \sqrt {{{\left( {\cos \frac{x}{2} - \sin \frac{x}{2}} \right)}^2}} }}{{\sqrt {{{\left( {\cos \frac{x}{2} + \sin \frac{x}{2}} \right)}^2}} - \sqrt {{{\left( {\cos \frac{x}{2} - \sin \frac{x}{2}} \right)}^2}} }}} \right]{/tex}   {tex}\left[ \because\sqrt {1 \pm \sin x} = \sqrt {{{\left( {\cos \frac{x}{2} \pm \sin \frac{x}{2}} \right)}^2}} \right] {/tex}

{tex}= {\cot ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\cos \frac{x}{2} + \sin \frac{x}{2} + \cos \frac{x}{2} - \sin \frac{x}{2}}}{{\cos \frac{x}{2} + \sin \frac{x}{2} - \cos \frac{x}{2} + \sin \frac{x}{2}}}{/tex}

{tex} = {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2\cos \frac{x}{2}}}{{2\sin \frac{x}{2}}}} \right){/tex}

{tex} = {\cot ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cot \frac{x}{2}} \right){/tex}

{tex} = \frac{x}{2}{/tex}

{tex}y = \frac{x}{2}{/tex}

{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Differentiate the function (log x) log x , x > 1, w.r.t to x.
question_html: Differentiate the function (log x)log x, x > 1, w.r.t to x.
solution_text: Let y = (log x) log x , x > 1 Taking logarithm on both sides {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log y = log (log x) log x = log x {tex}\times{/tex} log (log x) Differentiating both sides with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{y}} \frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}[\log \mathrm{x} \times \log (\log \mathrm{x})]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=\log (\log x) \times \frac{d}{d x}(\log x)+\log x \times \frac{d}{d x}[\log (\log x)]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=y\left[\log (\log x) \times \frac{1}{x}+\log x \times \frac{1}{\log x} \times \frac{d}{d x}(\log x)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=y\left[\frac{1}{x} \log (\log x)+\frac{1}{x}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=(\log x)^{\log x}\left[\frac{1}{x}+\frac{\log (\log x)}{x}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let y = (log x)log x, x > 1
Taking logarithm on both sides
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log y = log (log x)log x = log x {tex}\times{/tex} log (log x)
Differentiating both sides with respect to x, we get
{tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{y}} \frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}[\log \mathrm{x} \times \log (\log \mathrm{x})]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=\log (\log x) \times \frac{d}{d x}(\log x)+\log x \times \frac{d}{d x}[\log (\log x)]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=y\left[\log (\log x) \times \frac{1}{x}+\log x \times \frac{1}{\log x} \times \frac{d}{d x}(\log x)\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=y\left[\frac{1}{x} \log (\log x)+\frac{1}{x}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=(\log x)^{\log x}\left[\frac{1}{x}+\frac{\log (\log x)}{x}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Differentiate the function {tex}\cos \left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right){/tex} w.r.t x for some constant a and b.
question_html: Differentiate the function {tex}\cos \left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right){/tex} w.r.t x for some constant a and b.
solution_text: Let {tex}y = \cos \left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right){/tex} for some constants a and b {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \sin \left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \sin \left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right)\left( { - a\sin x + b\cos x} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \left( { - a\sin x + b\cos x} \right)\sin \left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \left( {a\sin x - b\cos x} \right)\sin \left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right){/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}y = \cos \left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right){/tex} for some constants a and b

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \sin \left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right)\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \sin \left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right)\left( { - a\sin x + b\cos x} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \left( { - a\sin x + b\cos x} \right)\sin \left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \left( {a\sin x - b\cos x} \right)\sin \left( {a\cos x + b\sin x} \right){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Differentiate the function (sin x – cos x (sin x – cos x) , {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}<x<\frac{3 \pi}{4}{/tex} w.r.t to x.
question_html: Differentiate the function (sin x – cos x(sin x – cos x), {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}<x<\frac{3 \pi}{4}{/tex} w.r.t to x.
solution_text: We have, y = (sin x - cos x) (sin x - cos x) , {tex} \frac { \pi } { 4 } < x < \frac { 3 \pi } { 4 }{/tex} , Therefore, on taking logarithm both sides, we get, log y = log (sin x - cos x) (sin x - cos x) , {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} log y= (sin x - cos x). log(sin x - cos x) Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t x, we get, {tex} \frac { 1 } { y } \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } = ( \sin x - \cos x ) \times{/tex} {tex} \frac { d } { d x } \log ( \sin x - \cos x ){/tex} + log(sin x - cos x) {tex} \times\frac { d } { d x } ( \sin x - \cos x ){/tex} [By using product rule of derivative] {tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 1 } { y } \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} = (sin x - cos x) {tex} \frac { 1 } { ( \sin x - \cos x ) }{/tex} {tex} \frac { d } { d x } ( \sin x - \cos x ){/tex} + log(sin x - cos x).(cos x + sin x) {tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 1 } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } = ( \sin x - \cos x ) \frac { 1 } { ( \sin x - \cos x ) }{/tex} (cos x + sin x) + log(sin x - cos x).(cos x + sin x) {tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 1 } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } = ( \cos x + \sin x ){/tex} +(cos x + sin x) + log(sin x - cos x) {tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} = y(cos x + sin x)[ 1+log(sin x - cos x)] {tex} \therefore \quad \frac { d y } { d x } {/tex} = (sin x - cos x) (sin x - cos x) (cos x + sin x)[ 1+log(sin x - cos x)]
solution_html:
We have, y = (sin x - cos x)(sin x - cos x), {tex} \frac { \pi } { 4 } < x < \frac { 3 \pi } { 4 }{/tex},

Therefore, on taking logarithm both sides, we get,
log y = log (sin x - cos x)(sin x - cos x),
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} log y= (sin x - cos x). log(sin x - cos x)
Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t x, we get,
{tex} \frac { 1 } { y } \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } = ( \sin x - \cos x ) \times{/tex}{tex} \frac { d } { d x } \log ( \sin x - \cos x ){/tex}+ log(sin x - cos x){tex} \times\frac { d } { d x } ( \sin x - \cos x ){/tex}[By using product rule of derivative]
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 1 } { y } \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex}= (sin x - cos x){tex} \frac { 1 } { ( \sin x - \cos x ) }{/tex}{tex} \frac { d } { d x } ( \sin x - \cos x ){/tex}+ log(sin x - cos x).(cos x + sin x)
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 1 } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } = ( \sin x - \cos x ) \frac { 1 } { ( \sin x - \cos x ) }{/tex}(cos x + sin x) + log(sin x - cos x).(cos x + sin x)
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 1 } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } = ( \cos x + \sin x ){/tex}+(cos x + sin x) + log(sin x - cos x)
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex}= y(cos x + sin x)[ 1+log(sin x - cos x)]
{tex} \therefore \quad \frac { d y } { d x } {/tex}= (sin x - cos x)(sin x - cos x)(cos x + sin x)[ 1+log(sin x - cos x)]

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Differentiate w.r.t. x the function in x x + x a + a x + a a , for some fixed a > 0 and x > 0.
question_html: Differentiate w.r.t. x the function in xx + xa + ax + aa , for some fixed a > 0 and x > 0.
solution_text: Let y = x x + x a + a x + a a , for some fixed a > 0 and x > 0 And let x x = u, x a = v a x = w and a a = s Then y = u + v + w + s {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}+\frac{d w}{d x}+\frac{d s}{d x}{/tex} ......(i) Now, u = x x Taking logarithm both sides, we get log u = log x x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log u = x log x Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{u}} \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\log \mathrm{x} \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{x} \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{x}){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=u\left[\log x+x \times \frac{1}{x}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=x^{x}[\log x+1]=x^{x}(1+\log x){/tex} ......(ii) v = x a Differentiating both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{a}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=a x^{a-1}{/tex} .....(iii) w = a x Taking logarithm both sides log w = log a x log w = x log a Differentiating both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{1}{w} \frac{d w}{d x}=\log a \times \frac{d}{d x}(x){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d w}{d x}=w \log a{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d w}{d x}=a^{x} \log a{/tex} ......(iv) s = a a Differentiating both sides with respect to x {tex}\frac{\mathrm{ds}}{\mathrm{dx}}=0{/tex} .....(v) Putting (ii), (iii), (iv) and (v) in (i) {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=x^{x}(1+\log x)+a x^{a-1}+a^{x} \log a+0{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=x^{x}(1+\log x)+a x^{a-1}+a^{x} \log a{/tex}
solution_html:
Let y = xx + xa + ax + aa, for some fixed a > 0 and x > 0
And let xx = u, xa = v ax = w and aa = s
Then y = u + v + w + s
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}+\frac{d w}{d x}+\frac{d s}{d x}{/tex} ......(i)
Now, 
u = xx
Taking logarithm both sides, we get
log u = log xx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log u = x log x
Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x
{tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{u}} \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\log \mathrm{x} \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{x} \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{x}){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=u\left[\log x+x \times \frac{1}{x}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=x^{x}[\log x+1]=x^{x}(1+\log x){/tex} ......(ii)
v = xa 
Differentiating both sides with respect to x
{tex}\frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{a}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=a x^{a-1}{/tex} .....(iii)
w = ax 
Taking logarithm both sides 
log w = log ax
log w = x log a
Differentiating both sides with respect to x
{tex}\frac{1}{w} \frac{d w}{d x}=\log a \times \frac{d}{d x}(x){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d w}{d x}=w \log a{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d w}{d x}=a^{x} \log a{/tex} ......(iv)
s = aa
Differentiating both sides with respect to x
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{ds}}{\mathrm{dx}}=0{/tex} .....(v) 
Putting (ii), (iii), (iv) and (v) in (i)
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=x^{x}(1+\log x)+a x^{a-1}+a^{x} \log a+0{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=x^{x}(1+\log x)+a x^{a-1}+a^{x} \log a{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Differentiate the function {tex}x^{x^{2}-3}+(x-3)^{x^{2}}, \text { for } x>3{/tex} w.r.t to x.
question_html: Differentiate the function {tex}x^{x^{2}-3}+(x-3)^{x^{2}}, \text { for } x>3{/tex} w.r.t to x.
solution_text: Let y = {tex}x^{x^{2}-3}+(x-3)^{x^{2}}{/tex} And let u = {tex}x^{x^{2}-3}{/tex} and v = {tex}(x-3)^{x^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} y = u + v Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} .....(i) Now, u = {tex}x^{x^{2}-3}{/tex} Taking logarithm both sides log u = log {tex}x^{x^{2}-3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log u = (x 2 - 3) log x Differentiating w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{u}} \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\log _{\mathrm{X}} \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}-3\right)+\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}-3\right) \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{x}){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=u\left[\log x \times 2 x+\left(x^{2}-3\right) \times \frac{1}{x}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=x^{x^{2}-3}\left[\frac{x^{2}-3}{x}+2 x \log x\right]{/tex} .....(ii) Also, v = {tex}(x-3)^{x^{2}}{/tex} Taking logarithm both sides log v = log {tex}(x-3)^{x^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log v = x 2 log(x-3) Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{v}} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\log (\mathrm{x}-3) \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}\right)+\mathrm{x}^{2} \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}[\log (\mathrm{x}-3)]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=v\left[\log (x-3) \times 2 x+x^{2} \times \frac{1}{(x-3)} \times \frac{d}{d x}(x-3)\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=(x-3)^{x^{2}}\left[2 x \log (x-3)+\frac{x^{2}}{(x-3)} \times 1\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=(x-3)^{x^{2}}\left[\frac{x^{2}}{(x-3)}+2 x \log (x-3)\right]{/tex} ....(iii) Substituting (ii) and (iii) in (i) {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=x^{x^{2}-3}\left[\frac{x^{2}-3}{x}+2 x \log x\right]{/tex} + {tex}(x-3)^{x^{2}}\left[\frac{x^{2}}{(x-3)}+2 x \log (x-3)\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let y = {tex}x^{x^{2}-3}+(x-3)^{x^{2}}{/tex}
And let u = {tex}x^{x^{2}-3}{/tex} and v = {tex}(x-3)^{x^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} y = u + v
Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x we get
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}.....(i)
Now,
u = {tex}x^{x^{2}-3}{/tex}
Taking logarithm both sides
log u = log {tex}x^{x^{2}-3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log u = (x2 - 3) log x
Differentiating w.r.t. x, we get
{tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{u}} \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\log _{\mathrm{X}} \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}-3\right)+\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}-3\right) \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}(\log \mathrm{x}){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=u\left[\log x \times 2 x+\left(x^{2}-3\right) \times \frac{1}{x}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=x^{x^{2}-3}\left[\frac{x^{2}-3}{x}+2 x \log x\right]{/tex}.....(ii)
Also,
v = {tex}(x-3)^{x^{2}}{/tex}
Taking logarithm both sides
log v = log {tex}(x-3)^{x^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}log v = x2log(x-3)
Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x
{tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{v}} \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\log (\mathrm{x}-3) \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{2}\right)+\mathrm{x}^{2} \times \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}[\log (\mathrm{x}-3)]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=v\left[\log (x-3) \times 2 x+x^{2} \times \frac{1}{(x-3)} \times \frac{d}{d x}(x-3)\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=(x-3)^{x^{2}}\left[2 x \log (x-3)+\frac{x^{2}}{(x-3)} \times 1\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=(x-3)^{x^{2}}\left[\frac{x^{2}}{(x-3)}+2 x \log (x-3)\right]{/tex}....(iii)
Substituting (ii) and (iii) in (i)
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=x^{x^{2}-3}\left[\frac{x^{2}-3}{x}+2 x \log x\right]{/tex} + {tex}(x-3)^{x^{2}}\left[\frac{x^{2}}{(x-3)}+2 x \log (x-3)\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} , if y = 12 (1 - cos t), x = 10 (t - sin t), {tex}-\frac{\pi}{2}<t<\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} , if y = 12 (1 - cos t), x = 10 (t - sin t), {tex}-\frac{\pi}{2}<t<\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
solution_text: Clearly, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\frac{d y}{d t}}{\frac{d x}{d t}}{/tex} Given, y = 12 (1 - cos t) ...(i) and, x = 10 (t - sin t) ...(ii) Differentiating (ii), with respect to t. {tex}\frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{d}{d t}[10(t-\sin t)]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d t}=10 \times \frac{d}{d t}(t-\sin t)=10(1-\cos t){/tex} Differentiating (i), with respect to t. {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}=\frac{d}{d t}[12(1-\cos t)]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d t}=12 \times \frac{d}{d t}(1-\cos t)=12 \times[0-(-\sin t)]=12 \sin t{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dt}}}{\frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{dt}}}=\frac{12 \sin \mathrm{t}}{10(1-\cos t)}=\frac{12 \times 2 \sin \frac{\mathrm{t}}{2} \cos \frac{\mathrm{t}}{2}}{10 \times 2 \sin ^{2} \frac{\mathrm{t}}{2}}=\frac{6}{5} \cot \frac{\mathrm{t}}{2}{/tex} {tex}or ~ \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{6}{5} \cot \frac{t}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Clearly, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\frac{d y}{d t}}{\frac{d x}{d t}}{/tex} 
Given, y = 12 (1 - cos t)  ...(i)
and, x = 10 (t - sin t)    ...(ii)
Differentiating (ii), with respect to t.
{tex}\frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{d}{d t}[10(t-\sin t)]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d t}=10 \times \frac{d}{d t}(t-\sin t)=10(1-\cos t){/tex} 
Differentiating (i),  with respect to t.
{tex}\frac{d y}{d t}=\frac{d}{d t}[12(1-\cos t)]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d t}=12 \times \frac{d}{d t}(1-\cos t)=12 \times[0-(-\sin t)]=12 \sin t{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dt}}}{\frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{dt}}}=\frac{12 \sin \mathrm{t}}{10(1-\cos t)}=\frac{12 \times 2 \sin \frac{\mathrm{t}}{2} \cos \frac{\mathrm{t}}{2}}{10 \times 2 \sin ^{2} \frac{\mathrm{t}}{2}}=\frac{6}{5} \cot \frac{\mathrm{t}}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}or ~ \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{6}{5} \cot \frac{t}{2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} , if y = sin –1 x + sin –1 {tex}\sqrt{1-x^{2}}, {/tex} 0 < x < 1
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}, if y = sin–1 x + sin–1 {tex}\sqrt{1-x^{2}}, {/tex} 0 < x < 1
solution_text: {tex}y = {\sin ^{ - 1}}x + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} {/tex} Differentiate both side w.r.t. x {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - \left( {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right)^2} }}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - \left( {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right)^2} }}.\frac{{1\left( { - 2x} \right)}}{{2\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - \left( {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right)^2} }}.\left( {\frac{{ - x}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2}} }}.\left( {\frac{{ - x}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \frac{1}{x}\left( {\frac{{ - x}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} - \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} = 0{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}y = {\sin ^{ - 1}}x + {\sin ^{ - 1}}\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} {/tex}
Differentiate both side w.r.t. x
{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - \left( {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right)^2} }}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - \left( {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right)^2} }}.\frac{{1\left( { - 2x} \right)}}{{2\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - \left( {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right)^2} }}.\left( {\frac{{ - x}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} \right){/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2}} }}.\left( {\frac{{ - x}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} \right){/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \frac{1}{x}\left( {\frac{{ - x}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}} \right){/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} - \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} = 0{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If {tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} + y\sqrt {1 + x} = 0{/tex} for -1<x<1 prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \frac{1}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}
question_html: If {tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} + y\sqrt {1 + x} = 0{/tex}  for -1<x<1 prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \frac{1}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} + y\sqrt {1 + x} = 0{/tex} {tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} = - y\sqrt {1 + x} {/tex} Squaring both sides, {tex}{x^2}\left( {1 + y} \right) = {y^2}\left( {1 + x} \right){/tex} {tex}{x^2} + {x^2}y = {y^2} + x{y^2}{/tex} {tex}{x^2} - {y^2} + {x^2}y - x{y^2} = 0{/tex} {tex}\left( {x - y} \right)\left( {x + y} \right) + xy\left( {x - y} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\left( {x - y} \right)\left[ {x + y + xy} \right] = 0{/tex} {tex}x + y + xy = 0{/tex} {tex}y\left( {1 + x} \right) = - x{/tex} {tex}y = \frac{{ - x}}{{1 + x}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \left[ {\frac{{\left( {1 + x} \right)\left( 1 \right) - \left( x \right)\left( 1 \right)}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= - \left[ {\frac{{1 + x - x}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} + y\sqrt {1 + x} = 0{/tex}

{tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} = - y\sqrt {1 + x} {/tex}

Squaring both sides,

{tex}{x^2}\left( {1 + y} \right) = {y^2}\left( {1 + x} \right){/tex}

{tex}{x^2} + {x^2}y = {y^2} + x{y^2}{/tex}

{tex}{x^2} - {y^2} + {x^2}y - x{y^2} = 0{/tex}

{tex}\left( {x - y} \right)\left( {x + y} \right) + xy\left( {x - y} \right) = 0{/tex}

{tex}\left( {x - y} \right)\left[ {x + y + xy} \right] = 0{/tex}

{tex}x + y + xy = 0{/tex}

{tex}y\left( {1 + x} \right) = - x{/tex}

{tex}y = \frac{{ - x}}{{1 + x}}{/tex}

{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \left[ {\frac{{\left( {1 + x} \right)\left( 1 \right) - \left( x \right)\left( 1 \right)}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}} \right]{/tex}{tex}= - \left[ {\frac{{1 + x - x}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If {tex}{\left( {x - a} \right)^2} + {\left( {y - b} \right)^2} = {c^2}{/tex} for some c > 0 prove that {tex}\frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}}}{/tex} is a constant independent of a and b.
question_html: If {tex}{\left( {x - a} \right)^2} + {\left( {y - b} \right)^2} = {c^2}{/tex} for some c > 0 prove that {tex}\frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}}}{/tex} is a constant independent of a and b.
solution_text: Given: {tex}{\left( {x - a} \right)^2} + {\left( {y - b} \right)^2} = {c^2},{/tex} ……….(i) {tex}\therefore 2\left( {x - a} \right) + 2\left( {y - b} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( {x - a} \right) = - 2\left( {y - b} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \left( {\frac{{x - a}}{{y - b}}} \right){/tex} ……….(ii) Again {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right).1 - \left( {x - a} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right).1 - \left( {x - a} \right)\left( {\frac{{ - \left( {x - a} \right)}}{{y - b}}} \right)} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} [From eq. (ii) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right) + \left( {\frac{{{{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}}}{{y - b}}} \right)} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - \left[ {{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - {c^2}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{/tex} ……….(iii) [using (i)] Putting values of {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}{/tex} in the given expression, {tex}\frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\frac{{\left( {x - a} \right)}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{ - {c^2}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{{\left[ {{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}} \times \frac{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{{ - {c^2}}} = \frac{{{{\left( {{c^2}} \right)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{ - {c^2}}} = - c{/tex} which is a constant and is independent of a and b.
solution_html:
Given: {tex}{\left( {x - a} \right)^2} + {\left( {y - b} \right)^2} = {c^2},{/tex}……….(i)

{tex}\therefore 2\left( {x - a} \right) + 2\left( {y - b} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 0{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( {x - a} \right) = - 2\left( {y - b} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \left( {\frac{{x - a}}{{y - b}}} \right){/tex} ……….(ii)

Again {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right).1 - \left( {x - a} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} 

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right).1 - \left( {x - a} \right)\left( {\frac{{ - \left( {x - a} \right)}}{{y - b}}} \right)} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} [From eq. (ii)

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right) + \left( {\frac{{{{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}}}{{y - b}}} \right)} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - \left[ {{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - {c^2}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{/tex} ……….(iii)  [using (i)]

Putting values of {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}{/tex} in the given expression,

{tex}\frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}}}{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\frac{{\left( {x - a} \right)}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{ - {c^2}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{{\left[ {{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}} \times \frac{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{{ - {c^2}}} = \frac{{{{\left( {{c^2}} \right)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{ - {c^2}}} = - c{/tex}

which is a constant and is independent of a and b.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If {tex}\cos y = x\cos \left( {a + y} \right){/tex} with {tex}\cos a \ne \pm 1{/tex} prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{{\sin a}}{/tex}
question_html: If {tex}\cos y = x\cos \left( {a + y} \right){/tex} with {tex}\cos a \ne \pm 1{/tex} prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{{\sin a}}{/tex}
solution_text: Given: {tex}\cos y = x\cos \left( {a + y} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x = \frac{{\cos y}}{{\cos \left( {a + y} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = \frac{d}{{dy}}\left( {\frac{{\cos y}}{{\cos \left( {a + y} \right)}}} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = \frac{{\cos \left( {a + y} \right)\frac{d}{{dy}}\cos y - \cos y\frac{d}{{dy}}\cos \left( {a + y} \right)}}{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = \frac{{\cos \left( {a + y} \right)\left( { - \sin y} \right) - \cos y\left\{ { - \sin \left( {a + y} \right)} \right\}}}{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - \cos \left( {a + y} \right)\sin y + sin\left( {a + y} \right)\cos y}}{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = \frac{{\sin \left( {a + y - y} \right)}}{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\sin a}}{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{{\sin a}}{/tex} [ reciprocal]
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\cos y = x\cos \left( {a + y} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow x = \frac{{\cos y}}{{\cos \left( {a + y} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = \frac{d}{{dy}}\left( {\frac{{\cos y}}{{\cos \left( {a + y} \right)}}} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = \frac{{\cos \left( {a + y} \right)\frac{d}{{dy}}\cos y - \cos y\frac{d}{{dy}}\cos \left( {a + y} \right)}}{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = \frac{{\cos \left( {a + y} \right)\left( { - \sin y} \right) - \cos y\left\{ { - \sin \left( {a + y} \right)} \right\}}}{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - \cos \left( {a + y} \right)\sin y + sin\left( {a + y} \right)\cos y}}{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = \frac{{\sin \left( {a + y - y} \right)}}{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{\sin a}}{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{{{\cos }^2}\left( {a + y} \right)}}{{\sin a}}{/tex} [ reciprocal]

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If x = a (cos t + t sin t) and y = a (sin t – t cos t), find {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}{/tex}
question_html: If x = a (cos t + t sin t) and y = a (sin t – t cos t), find {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}{/tex}
solution_text: x = a(cos t + t.sin t) {tex}\frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = a\left[ { - \sin t + t\cos t + \sin t.1} \right]{/tex} {tex}\frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = a\left( {t.\cos t} \right){/tex} ...(1) y = a(sin t - tcos t) {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = a\left[ {\cos t - \left( { - t\sin t + \cos t.1} \right)} \right]{/tex} = a( cos t +t sin t - cos t) = a(t sin t) {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{a\,t.\sin t}}{{a\,t.\cos t}} = \tan t{/tex} {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\tan t} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {\tan t.} \right)\frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}= {\sec ^2}t.\frac{1}{{a\,t.\cos t}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{a\,t.{{\cos }^3}t}}{/tex}
solution_html:
x = a(cos t + t.sin t)
{tex}\frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = a\left[ { - \sin t + t\cos t + \sin t.1} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = a\left( {t.\cos t} \right){/tex} ...(1)
y = a(sin t - tcos t)
{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = a\left[ {\cos t - \left( { - t\sin t + \cos t.1} \right)} \right]{/tex}
= a( cos t +t sin t - cos t)
= a(t sin t)
{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{a\,t.\sin t}}{{a\,t.\cos t}} = \tan t{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\tan t} \right){/tex}
{tex}= \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {\tan t.} \right)\frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex}
{tex}= {\sec ^2}t.\frac{1}{{a\,t.\cos t}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{{a\,t.{{\cos }^3}t}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If f(x) = | x | 3 , show that f ″(x) exists for all real x and find it.
question_html: If f(x) = | x |3 , show that f ″(x) exists for all real x and find it.
solution_text: We know that, {tex}|x|=\left\{\begin{array}{r} {x, \text { if } x \geq 0} \\ {-x, \text { if } x<0} \end{array}\right.{/tex} When, x {tex}\ge{/tex} 0, Then f(x) = |x| 3 = x 3 {tex}\implies{/tex} f’(x) = 3x 2 And {tex}f^{\prime\prime}(x)=6x{/tex} When x < 0, Then, f(x) = |x| 3 = ( – x) 3 = – x 3 {tex}\implies{/tex} {tex}f^\prime(x)=-3x^2{/tex} , And, {tex}f^{\prime\prime}(x)=-6x{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}f^{\prime\prime}(x){/tex} = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{r} {6 x, x \geq 0} \\ {-6 x, x<0} \end{array}\right.{/tex}
solution_html:
We know that,
{tex}|x|=\left\{\begin{array}{r} {x, \text { if } x \geq 0} \\ {-x, \text { if } x<0} \end{array}\right.{/tex}
When, x {tex}\ge{/tex} 0,   
Then f(x) = |x|3 = x3
{tex}\implies{/tex} f’(x) = 3x2
And {tex}f^{\prime\prime}(x)=6x{/tex}
When x < 0,
Then, f(x) = |x|3 = ( – x)3 = – x3
{tex}\implies{/tex}{tex}f^\prime(x)=-3x^2{/tex},
And, {tex}f^{\prime\prime}(x)=-6x{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}f^{\prime\prime}(x){/tex} = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{r} {6 x, x \geq 0} \\ {-6 x, x<0} \end{array}\right.{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Using the fact that {tex}\sin \left( {A + B} \right) = \sin A\cos B + \cos A\sin B{/tex} and the differentiation, obtain the sum formula for cosines.
question_html: Using the fact that {tex}\sin \left( {A + B} \right) = \sin A\cos B + \cos A\sin B{/tex} and the differentiation, obtain the sum formula for cosines.
solution_text: Given: {tex}\sin \left( {A + B} \right) = \sin A\cos B + \cos A\sin B{/tex} Consider, A and B as function of t and differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, {tex}\cos \left( {A + B} \right)\left( {\frac{{dA}}{{dt}} + \frac{{dB}}{{dt}}} \right) = \sin A\left( { - \sin B} \right)\frac{{dB}}{{dt}} + \cos B\left( {\cos A\frac{{dA}}{{dt}}} \right){/tex} {tex} + \cos A\cos B\frac{{dB}}{{dt}} + \sin B\left( { - \sin A} \right)\frac{{dA}}{{dt}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left( {A + B} \right)\left( {\frac{{dA}}{{dB}} + \frac{{dB}}{{dt}}} \right) = \left( {\cos A\cos B - \sin A\sin B} \right)\left( {\frac{{dA}}{{dt}} + \frac{{dB}}{{dt}}} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left( {A + B} \right) = \left( {\cos A\cos B - \sin A\sin B} \right){/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\sin \left( {A + B} \right) = \sin A\cos B + \cos A\sin B{/tex} 

Consider, A and B as function of t and differentiating both sides w.r.t. x,

{tex}\cos \left( {A + B} \right)\left( {\frac{{dA}}{{dt}} + \frac{{dB}}{{dt}}} \right) = \sin A\left( { - \sin B} \right)\frac{{dB}}{{dt}} + \cos B\left( {\cos A\frac{{dA}}{{dt}}} \right){/tex}{tex} + \cos A\cos B\frac{{dB}}{{dt}} + \sin B\left( { - \sin A} \right)\frac{{dA}}{{dt}}{/tex} 

{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left( {A + B} \right)\left( {\frac{{dA}}{{dB}} + \frac{{dB}}{{dt}}} \right) = \left( {\cos A\cos B - \sin A\sin B} \right)\left( {\frac{{dA}}{{dt}} + \frac{{dB}}{{dt}}} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \left( {A + B} \right) = \left( {\cos A\cos B - \sin A\sin B} \right){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Does there exist a function which is continuous everywhere but not differentiable at exactly two points? Justify your answer.
question_html: Does there exist a function which is continuous everywhere but not differentiable at exactly two points? Justify your answer.
solution_text: Consider the function f (x) = |x| + |x - 1| f is continuous everywhere but it is not differentiable at x = 0 and x = 1.
solution_html:
Consider the function f (x) = |x| + |x - 1|
f is continuous everywhere but it is not differentiable at x = 0 and x = 1.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If {tex}y = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f(x)}&{g(x)}&{h(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right|{/tex} ,Prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f'(x)}&{g'(x)}&{h'(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right|{/tex}
question_html: If {tex}y = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f(x)}&{g(x)}&{h(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right|{/tex},Prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f'(x)}&{g'(x)}&{h'(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right|{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}y = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f(x)}&{g(x)}&{h(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f'(x)}&{g'(x)}&{h'(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right| + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f(x)}&{g(x)}&{h(x)} \\ 0&0&0 \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex} + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f(x)}&{g(x)}&{h(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ 0&0&0 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f'(x)}&{g'(x)}&{h'(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right| + 0 + 0{/tex} {tex} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f'(x)}&{g'(x)}&{h'(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right|{/tex} . Hence proved
solution_html:
{tex}y = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f(x)}&{g(x)}&{h(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f'(x)}&{g'(x)}&{h'(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right| + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f(x)}&{g(x)}&{h(x)} \\ 0&0&0 \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right|{/tex}{tex} + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f(x)}&{g(x)}&{h(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ 0&0&0 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f'(x)}&{g'(x)}&{h'(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right| + 0 + 0{/tex}
{tex} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {f'(x)}&{g'(x)}&{h'(x)} \\ l&m&n \\ a&b&c \end{array}} \right|{/tex}.

Hence proved

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If y = e a cos-1 x , – 1 ≤ x ≤ 1, show that (1 - x 2 ) {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} - a 2 y = 0.
question_html: If y = ea cos-1 x, – 1 ≤ x ≤ 1, show that (1 - x2) {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} - a2y = 0.
solution_text: {tex}y = {e^{a{{\cos }^{ - 1}}x}}{/tex} Differentiating both sides w.r.t x, {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = {e^{{{\ acos }^{ - 1}}x}}.\frac{-a}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{/tex} {tex}\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} \frac{{{d}y}}{{dx}} = - {e^{a{{\cos }^{ - 1}}x}}.a{/tex} Differentiating both sides w.r.t x, {tex}\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{dx^2}} + \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\frac{{\left( { - 2x} \right)}}{{2\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} = - a{e^{a{{\cos }^{ - 1}}x}}a\frac{{\left( { - 1} \right)}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{/tex} {tex}\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{dx^2}} - \frac{x}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{{a^2}{e^{{{acos }^{ - 1}}x}}}}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{/tex} {tex}\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} - x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = {a^2}{e^{{{acos }^{ - 1}}x}}{/tex} {tex}\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} - x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = a^2y{/tex} {tex}\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} -x \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} - {a^2}.y = 0{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}y = {e^{a{{\cos }^{ - 1}}x}}{/tex}

Differentiating both sides w.r.t x,

{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = {e^{{{\ acos }^{ - 1}}x}}.\frac{-a}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{/tex}
